title: Serena Knowledgebase - Alfeventmanager.log: Error connecting to mail server for address : SBMEventUser@localhost
content:
Article ID: D17723
Defect ID: DEF231500
Product: SBM 10.1.2
Found In: Development Manager 4.0.1 Development Manager 4.5 Release Control 4.5 Release Manager 4.0.1 Release Manager 4.5 Requirements Manager 4.0.1 Requirements Manager 4.5 Service Manager 4.5
Fixed In: Development Manager 4.5 Release Control 4.5 Release Manager 4.5 Requirements Manager 4.5 SBM 10.1.2.1 Service Manager 4.5

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The alfeventmanager.log displays the error below, and there is no way prevent the connection from happening.
ERROR [mailto-Worker-17] [org.apache.axis2.transport.mail.MailTransportListenerEx] [::] -- Error connecting to mail server for address : SBMEventUser@localhost
javax.mail.MessagingException: Connect failed;
nested exception is:
java.net.ConnectException: Connection refused: connect
The Configurator is always setting these values even when the Application Repository/Event Manager pop3 mail settings are turned off.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Common Scheduler shuts down if DB server is cycled - Failed to obtain DB connection from data source 'myDS': java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' ... Could not create connection
content:
Article ID: D13450
Defect ID: DEF175841
Product: SBM 2009 R1.03

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Run Application Repository
2. Recycle your Database server so that all connections are dropped
3. Notice that error messages will start appearing in the log regarding lost connections from common scheduler.
Actual Result:
The following messages will appear in server.log (or maybe Mashupmgr.log) and all activities will fail to run.
org.quartz.JobPersistenceException: Failed to obtain DB connection from data source 'myDS': java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' org.jboss.util.NestedSQLException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.); - nested throwable: (org.jboss.resource.JBossResourceException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.)) [See nested exception: java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' org.jboss.util.NestedSQLException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.); - nested throwable: (org.jboss.resource.JBossResourceException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.))]
Expected Result:
Common Scheduler should grab a new connection from the pool if it detects that it lost a connection.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim CM 14.1.0.3: DMDEPLOYSRV: 'Unable to connect to database' with 'Failed to get a new connection from pool'
content:
Article ID: D20961
Defect ID: DEF256461
Product: Dimensions CM 14.2
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 14.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Following a restart of the Dimensions listener deployment jobs started to stall with just the status "Submitted" when viewed in the Deployment tab of Web Client. When the SDP trace file for the dmdeploysrv.exe process was checked there was a message indicating that the process had failed to connect to the database to scan for jobs because it failed to get a new connection from the pool. The problem was that the listener was going down but not waiting for all the child processes to stop. The restart of the listener generated a whole new set of processes when viewed in Task Manager but nothing would connect.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
All the Dimensions processes need to be stopped in this situation before the listener can be restarted again and the deployment server should connect and scan for jobs to run.

DEF256461

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Local IP addresses for connections from File Server clients running on the server are sometimes treated as remote
content:
Article ID: D23867
Defect ID: DEF266043
Product: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.2
Fixed In: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
On systems with multiple IP addresses, including mixed IPv4 / IPv6 configurations, Version Manager sometimes does not detect that a client was local to the server if the connection uses an IP address other than localhost.
Depending on the File Server configuration, this can affect the ability to run the File Server Admin utility as well as running readdb, makedb, vsplit and vtransfer.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This has been fixed in VM 8.5.3.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim cm 14.4 : installation on Centos with local postgres fails with this message "dm_psql: could not connect to server: Connection refused"
content:
Article ID: S142531

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
14.4 installation fails at this step :
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Dimensions CM 14.4 - PostgreSQL Database connection details
Please enter PostgreSQL connection parameters.
PostgreSQL hostname: [localhost] <hostname>
PostgreSQL port: [5432]
PostgreSQL Database name: [dim14]
PostgreSQL User name: [postgres]
PostgreSQL password:
Press 1 for Next, 2 for Previous, 3 to Cancel or 5 to Redisplay [1]
dm_psql: could not connect to server: Connection refused Is the server running on host "<hostname>" (<ip address>) and accepting TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
[OK]
what is causing this ? stating that :
1_ port 5432 is opened at firewall
2_ login to postgres works
3_ pg_hba.conf file is set correctly following installation requirements described page 60 of unix installation guide

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
In postgres.conf file (located in same directory that pg_hba.conf file),
listen_addresses
parameter is set to localhost by default.
Adding the ip address of the Centos machine to this parameter then stopping/starting postgres fixes the issue when restarting the installation

title: Serena Knowledgebase - ERROR -- Failed to send notification email to recipient < email address >: Could not connect to SMTP host: localhost, port: 25
content:
Article ID: S140347

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
You may see the following error in your deploy log even if you deploy was successful,
ERROR -- Failed to send notification email to recipient < email address > : Could not connect to SMTP host: localhost, port: 25

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
If your notification server is running and you have verified that the port is listening and notifications are successfully sent, then you may have a configuration issue.
Launch SBM Configurator
Select the Mail Services tab under Standard Settings
Select the Application Repository and Event Manager tab
Check the box to 'Use SMTP Mail Server for the Event Manager and Application Repository'.
Enter the connection information for the SMTP server used by the Event Manager and Application Repository.
Hint: the host and port are the same configuration setup for your Notification Server.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Error: Failed to connect to the Dimensions Server! Error: Connection refused by (server host name) (10061) Check Status of Dimensions Network Daemon on and attempt reconnection
content:
Article ID: S121866

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
DIM: Error: Failed to connect to the Dimensions Server! Error:
Connection refused by (server host name) (10061) Check Status of Dimensions
Network Daemon on and attempt reconnection

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Check the following points:
UNIX

1) Check to see if the Dimensions Network process is running and is owned by Root.

PCMS_DFS pcms_lc_accesd
ps -ef | grep pcms_lc_accesd

PCMS_SDP Dimensions_listener
ps -ef |grep dimensions_listener

2) Verify that the Network Administration Tool has been correctly updated to Register the DFS Node.
a) Check "Operating System Registration".
b) Check "Network client and server connections".
c) Check the "Network Node Registration".
c.1) If this information is filled out, select the "Network Node" and then select the "Network Objects" and make sure the information is filled out; in otherwords, ensure that pcms_dfs "tcp" or pcms_sdp "standard dimensions protocol" has been entered properly.

3) Confirm the Port Numbers in the "Services" file and the PCMS.CFG file.

a) The port numbers for PCMS_DFS and PCMS_SDP:

PCMS_DFS should default to 670/tcp in both files
PCMS_SDP should default to 671/tcp in both files.

If a different port is being used, make sure its the same in both files.

b) also ensure that services contains LISTENER with a default port of 1521/tcp.

4) In the PCMS_STARTUP script ensure the DFS lines are uncommented.

5) NOTE: ensure there are no TRACE logs enabled in the pcms_startup script

WINDOWS

1) Check to see if the Dimensions services are running:

PVCS Dimensions services (pcms_dfs)
PVCS Dimensions listener services (pcms_sdp)

2) Verify that the Network Administration Tool has been correctly updated to Register the DFS Node.
a) Check "Operating System Registration".
b) Check "Network client and server connections".
c) Check the "Network Node Registration".
c.1) If this information is filled out, select the "Network Node" and then select the "Network Objects" and make sure the information is filled out; in otherwords, ensure that pcms_dfs "tcp" or pcms_sdp "standard dimensions protocol" has been entered properly.

3) Confirm the Port Numbers in the PCMS.CFG file from de server and client.

The port numbers for PCMS_DFS and PCMS_SDP:

PCMS_SERVICE_PCMS_DFS_TCP should default to 670
PCMS_SERVICE_PCMS_SDP_TCP should default to 671

If a different port is being used, make sure its the same in both files.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Error getting SLA data: Connection Refused: Connect when clicking on a service icon in Request Center.
content:
Article ID: S140722

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When you are in Request Center and you click on one of the Service Icons you may see the following error.
"Error getting SLA data: Connection Refused: Connect"
This error can occur when using an HTTPS url to connect to Request Center.
When you click on the icon in Request Center the SLA data is retrieved from JBoss. If you open the SBM Configurator and go to the JBoss section and verify there is a certificate issued for JBoss. If there isn't a certificate assigned you can create one in the Configurator tool or assign your own. The other option is to not use https for JBoss. In order to do that remove the port value for the https field in the JBoss Server tab and uncheck the use https checkbox on the Component Server tab in the JBoss section. After clicking Apply this will force JBoss to always connect via http and you won't see this error in Request Center.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SSMMAIL getting connect failed RC= 61  reason=  ECONNREFUSED Connection refused
content:
Article ID: S141229

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
SSMMAIL notification
fails sending sending to an SMTP server. The SSMMAIL task is getting the following error message as seen below:
connect failed RC= 61
reason=
ECONNREFUSED Connection refused
-------
CALLING HPSNOTFY
RETURN FROM CALL
Mailing to: <xyz@abc.local>
socket:
CALL 0 1
Setsockopt:
CALL 0
connect failed RC= 61
reason=
ECONNREFUSED Connection refused
PARM=
Û{ X=
000154C0
PARSED MESSAGE INFORMATION
==========================
MSGLEN=125
DATE=
TIME=
MEMBER=
EVENT=
JOBNAME=
PROGRAM=
USERID=
DSNAME=
VOLSER=
NEWNAME=
LPAR=
EVENT=
Û{
CALLING HPSNOTFY
SUBSYSTEM ID =SERH

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Solution:
A TCPSMPD Daemon (server) was down on the LPARS where the task was failing.
To test, change SMTPSERVER= parm in MAIL4NFY value to an IP address were it works.
----------------- ERRNO=61 ECONNREFUSED meaning --------------
1) General:
Connection refused generally means that the Server did not accept the connect-request. Or, it could be that the Server has not successfully started yet.
2)
This error occurs on a CONNECT socket call when the connection to another host fails because no application is listening on the port that your application is trying to connect to.
Analysis:
- Determine whether the application you are connecting to has issued a LISTEN for the port to which the client application is attempting to connect

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Invalid Application Engine Host Name in Oracle Sample Database
content:
Article ID: D26159
Defect ID: DEF324513
Product: SBM 11.5

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The Application Engine host name defaults to
orbmpd-96651bec' instead of the actual Application Engine host name when using the Oracle sample database with SBM 11.5.
For example: http://orbmpd-96651bec/workcenter

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
To w
ork around this problem:
1) Log in to Application Repository
2) Open the Environments tab.
3) Select "Default Environment"
4) Change the link to the actual Application Engine host name

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim CM 14.5: DBI4502374E SQL-904(000001F18FF84740) ORA-00904: "RU"."USER_NAME": invalid identifier
content:
Article ID: D26471
Defect ID: DEF328837
Product: Dimensions CM 14.5
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 14.5.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
An attempt to relate an item to a request in Desktop Client generates the error
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
DBI4502374E SQL-904(000001F18FF84740) ORA-00904: "RU"."USER_NAME": invalid identifier
PRG4504102E Failed to query matching items
The process followed is:
-Relate/Unrelate for a Request
-Add Item Relationship
-Find
-Selection of Product and Project
-Click on "Find now":
=>Message: "Error: - Query Aborted!"
This is caused by having DM_PRODUCT_USER defined in dm.cfg.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
As a workaround until a fix is available you can relate the items via Drag & Drop or a check-out operation.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Application Engine database log contains "ORA-00904: "DBMS_CRYPTO"."HASH": invalid identifier" message
content:
Article ID: D22018
Defect ID: DEF273439
Product: SBM 11.0
Found In: Service Manager 5.2.1
Fixed In: SBM 11.0 Service Manager 5.2.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After you complete the Application Engine database upgrade to SBM 11.0, the following message may appear in the database upgrade log for Oracle systems:
"ORA-00904: "DBMS_CRYPTO"."HASH": invalid identifier"
The Application Engine upgrade could not access the DBMS_CRYPTO library to add SHA-1 hashes for resource files to the TS_BLOBS table. This is not an error, as these hashes are eventually created at runtime if they are not added now. However, some upgrade steps, deploy steps, and IIS startup may be temporarily slower until the Application Engine creates these hashes. Alternatively, your DBA can invoke the following SQL manually to add the hashes now:
merge into TS_BLOBS b
using (select bh.TS_ID blobid,rawtohex(DBMS_CRYPTO.hash( bh.TS_BLOB, 3)) h from TS_BLOBS bh join TS_RESOURCES r on r.TS_BLOBID=bh.TS_ID and bh.TS_BLOB is not null and bh.TS_HASH is null) r
on (b.TS_ID=r.blobid)
when matched then update set b.TS_HASH=r.h

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The ORA-00904 error is due to a change in SBM 11.0 that allows Application Engine to compare file resources from the database to the file system using a SHA-1 hash of the file contents. If you do not have access to the DBMS_CRYPTO library during upgrade, this can cause slower behavior in another step of the upgrade, when SBM puts files into the database. It can also cause slower behavior on IIS startup, when SBM gets files from the database. However, the runtime will eventually update the SHA-1 values in the database and the missing DBMS_CRYPTO privileges do not have a lasting impact on performance. Therefore if possible, prior to the upgrade, consult your DBA and consider granting access to the
DBMS_CRYPTO library
to get a jump-start on hashing the blobs on upgrade or execute the above SQL if this message appears.

DEF273439

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  When trying to recreate indexes, an error of ORA-02222: invalid PCTINCREASE storage option value occurs
content:
Article ID: S113865

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When trying to recreate indexes, an error of
ORA-02222: invalid PCTINCREASE storage option value occurs

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
When trying to recreate indexes, an error of ORA-02222: invalid PCTINCREASE
storage option value occurs when running the create_pcms_indices.sql.
The workaround is to edit the sql script so that the pctincrease is not set
to NULL but set to either 0 or 1. This will need to be throughout the
script.
Example:
ENABLE CONSTRAINT PK_BLD_VALUES_IN_GROUP USING
INDEX
storage (initial
131K
next
6K
maxextents
2147483645
minextents
1
pctincrease
0)
pctfree
10;

title: Serena Knowledgebase - ORA 00922 MISSING OR INVALID OPTION when creating a project
content:
Article ID: S104679

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
ORA 00922 MISSING OR INVALID OPTION when creating a project

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
A number of things to check.
1) Check to see if you are using a project name with a non alpha
numeric character that Oracle doesn't support. Create a
project with a database name with only alpha numeric characters
2) And
make sure it passes oracle name length rules.
3) Check to see if the user has privileges to create tables in Oracle
database. The user in this situation is the DBMS ADMIN user in the Server
Definition Wizard. It is not optional to fill this in for a Tracker
Administrator.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9: Rebuilding Indexes, get: ORA-01630: max # extents (121) reached in temp segment in tablespace PCMS_TEMP
content:
Article ID: S119593

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
DIM: Rebuilding Indexes, get: ORA-01630: max # extents (121) reached in
temp segment in tablespace PCMS_TEMP

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Correct by changing default storage parameters on temporary
tablespace.
The error message indicates that Oracle was unable to
allocate a new extent in a temp segment as it was creating the indexes. The
reason the new segment could not be created was because the structure in use was
at the maximum number of segments (121).
As indexes are created, Oracle
uses space in the temp tablespace (usually named PCMS_TEMP) for temporary
storage or sorts, selects and some indexes. Dimensions cannot directly specify
the storage parameters for the temp tablespace. All temporary objects created in
the temp tablespace inherit the default storage parameters associated with the
tablespace itself. Therefore, to correct the problem, we would alter the default
storage clause on the temporary tablespace.
To calculate the best set of
storage parameters, I usually divide the total space available in the temp
tablespace by 100 and use that as my initial and next sizes. The reason for this
is that Oracle will be able to use all of the TEMP tablespace for one object
containing approximately 100 extents. In this way, we are sure that the temp
tablespace can be used efficiently.
Therefore, if you have a PCMS_TEMP
tablespace with a total of 334M you would use the following statements: (note
the rounding upward of extent size)
sqlplus
system/<password>
SQL> alter tablespace PCMS_TEMP default
storage (initial 4M next 4M);
SQL> exit
After this you can attempt
to recreate the index in question. If it fails please send the error message, a
PCMS_SIZING report, and an Oracle_sizing report.

[content]

temp segment in tablespace PCMS_TEMP

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim14:  Upgrade:  SQL-1408(00000000024AECE0) ORA-01408: such column list already indexed Error detected in C:\Serena\Dimensions14.1\CM\dbms\upgrade\upgradeb_141.sql, line 2114
content:
Article ID: S140901

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When running the DMDBA upgrade command, an error of:
SQL-1408(00000000024AECE0) ORA-01408: such column list already indexed Error detected in C:\Serena\Dimensions14.1\CM\dbms\upgrade\upgradeb_141.sql, line 2114
Note: The line number can vary.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This error can occur if there are other indexes within the database which already have the columns indexed.
For instance, in looking at the upgradedb_141.sql script, it can be verified which index that is attempting to be created and the tablename. In the above example, the indexes on the table of area_catalogue were attempting to be created.
Obtaining a list of indexes for the table in question, can be done through SQLPlus as the system user similar to the following:
select * from dba_ind_columns where table_owner = 'CM_TYPICAL' and table_name = 'AREA_CATALOGUE';
This will provide a list of indexes for the tablename noted. In turn, dropping the indexes which have the columns to which the new indexes are being built for and re-running the upgrade command will allow the upgrade to proceed.

Note: The line number can vary.

Solutions
Dim14: Upgrade: SQL-1408(00000000024AECE0) ORA-01408: such column list already indexed Error detected in C:\Serena\Dimensions14.1\CM\dbms\upgrade\upgradeb_141.sql, line 2114
Back
View all Solutions
Rate this Page
ID:
S140901
Published:
15 December 2014
Product(s)
Dimensions CM
Description
When running the DMDBA upgrade command, an error of:
SQL-1408(00000000024AECE0) ORA-01408: such column list already indexed Error detected in C:\Serena\Dimensions14.1\CM\dbms\upgrade\upgradeb_141.sql, line 2114
Note: The line number can vary.
Resolution
This error can occur if there are other indexes within the database which already have the columns indexed.
For instance, in looking at the upgradedb_141.sql script, it can be verified which index that is attempting to be created and the tablename. In the above example, the indexes on the table of area_catalogue were attempting to be created.
Obtaining a list of indexes for the table in question, can be done through SQLPlus as the system user similar to the following:
select * from dba_ind_columns where table_owner = 'CM_TYPICAL' and table_name = 'AREA_CATALOGUE';
This will provide a list of indexes for the tablename noted. In turn, dropping the indexes which have the columns to which the new indexes are being built for and re-running the upgrade command will allow the upgrade to proceed.
Applies To
Dimensions CM

14.1
Product Fixed In
Dimensions CM
Release Fixed In
14.1

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Problem:    Desired Oracle Tablespace does not show in Tracker Admin selection list
content:
Article ID: S122866

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When creating a new project with Tracker Administrator, the desired tablespace does not show up in the selection list. An error message also may occur of:
ORA-00922 Missing or invalid option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The selection list is populated with ONLY the tablespaces that the login user has an available quota on. To ensure that the proper table space appears, make sure that the user that is logged in has an available quota.
Quotas can be altered similar to the following.
User : TestUser
Tablespace : TestTableSpace
ALTER USER "TestUser" QUOTA UNLIMITED ON TestTableSpace;
This will give the user unlimited quota on the appropriate tablespace.
Keywords: Oracle, Tablespace, Quota
Keven Koski -- PVCS Answerline

title: Serena Knowledgebase - ResMan server schema failed during upgrade of Common Services, but no error was logged during the upgrade
content:
Article ID: D23434
Defect ID: DEF289800
Product: SBM 11.0.1
Fixed In: SBM 11.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During the upgrade itself, there are no errors logged when the ResMan (Resource Manager), work center or SLS schemas are upgraded. If there are any problems during the upgrade, you will not see the errors until you try to log into work center.
After the upgrade is complete and you try to log into work center, users will see "Unable to validate user, please contact your Administrator" and errors such as the following will be in different logs. However it is very difficult to resolve the problem based solely on these errors.
== Tomcat.log ==
2016-08-09 02:28:26,305 ERROR [localhost-startStop-2] [org.apache.catalina.core.ContainerBase.[Catalina].[localhost].[/commonsvc]] [::] -- Exception sending context initialized event to listener instance of class org.springframework.web.context.ContextLoaderListener
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'org.springframework.scheduling.support.ScheduledMethodRunnable#0': Cannot resolve reference to bean 'invalidateCacheProcess' while setting constructor argument; nested exception is org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'dbUpgrader': Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is com.serena.core.runtime.CoreException: Failed upgrading ResMan server schema.
== server.log ==
2016-08-09 02:28:26,260 ERROR [localhost-startStop-2] [org.springframework.web.context.ContextLoader] [::] -- Context initialization failed
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'org.springframework.scheduling.support.ScheduledMethodRunnable#0': Cannot resolve reference to bean 'invalidateCacheProcess' while setting constructor argument; nested exception is org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'dbUpgrader': Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is com.serena.core.runtime.CoreException: Failed upgrading ResMan server schema.
== resourceMgmt.log ==
INFO 27-06-2016 14:05:13 [RsmDbUpgrader] -- Schema for ResMan Server does not exist in database - creating it....
INFO 27-06-2016 14:05:13 [RsmDbUpgrader] -- Schema for ResMan server was created
ERROR 07-07-2016 03:19:34 [RsmDbUpgrader] -- ResMan server failed to initialize.
ERROR 07-07-2016 03:19:34 [RsmDbUpgrader] -- Exception: Failed upgrading ResMan server schema.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
See L3R06393.

[content]
DEF289800

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Errors appear when JBoss starts on the Orchestration Engine server if Mashup Manager (Application Administrator) is not installed on that server
content:
Article ID: D10297
Defect ID: DEF184992
Product: SBM 2009 R2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.1 Development Manager 1.2 Release Control 3.2 Release Control 3.3 Release Manager 2.0 Requirements Manager 1.0 SBM 10.1 Service Manager 3.0

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
A JBoss instance that runs the orchestration engine throws errors each time Jboss is started.
: ERROR 2010-05-21 12:30:51,932 STDERR -- LoggerWrapper.loadLogger: Loaded Common Logger class: com.serena.eventlog.internal.logger.LoggerImpl
: INFO 2010-05-21 12:30:51,963 mashupmgr.EventLog.Server -- Initializing event log...
: ERROR 2010-05-21 12:30:51,963 STDERR -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
: ERROR 2010-05-21 12:30:51,963 STDERR -- Exception: serenaAdminDsn not bound
: FATAL 2010-05-21 12:30:51,963 mashupmgr.EventLog.Server -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
: FATAL 2010-05-21 12:30:51,963 mashupmgr.EventLog.Server -- Exception: serenaAdminDsn not bound
Note the following important details:
This is only a problem when Mashup Manager (also known as Application Administrator) is not installed on the same server as the Event Manager or Orchestration Engine.
When this issue occurs, no event messages appear in the event log for the SBM environment that uses that JBoss server.
Errors appear in the server.log when JBoss is started.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - mail2exe failing with s0c4
content:
Article ID: D14557
Defect ID: DEF214274
Product: ChangeMan SSM 8.3.4

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When starting the MAIL2EXE task, a S0C4 occurs.
1IKJ56644I NO VALID TSO USERID, DEFAULT USER ATTRIBUTES USED
ACF0C038 ACF2 LOGONID ATTRIBUTES HAVE REPLACED DEFAULT USER ATTRIBUTES
READY
%MAIL2EXE
Debugging strings ON
BEGAN SCANNING RTCT NOTIFY LOG AT 10:23:32 2012/01/31
Event types which trigger notification:
Action=Add,Upd,Del,Sta,Ren
Data set patterns which trigger notification:
DSN=SYS1.PARMLIB
NOTIFY=susan_z@xyz.org
MEM=*
EXCLID=TSOID1 TSOID2
DSN=SYS1.PROCLIB
NOTIFY=susan_z@xyz.org,mary_e@xyz.org
MEM=*
EXCLID=TSOID1 TSOID2
DSN=SYS1.LINKLIB
NOTIFY=susan_z@xyz.org
MEM=*
EXCLID=TSOID1 TSOID2
DSN=SYS1.LPALIB
NOTIFY=susan_z@xyz.org
MEM=*
EXCLID=TSOID1 TSOID2
initialize: CALL 0 DRMMAIL 4 *INET
socketsetlist : CALL 0 DRMMAIL
CALLING= 1
System abend code 0C4, reason code 00000016.
Abend in host command HPSNOTFY or address environment routine LINKPGM.
539 *-* address linkpgm 'HPSNOTFY PARM HPSVAL'
+++ RC(-196) +++
Return from initial HPSNOTFY CALL
PARM= AAAA X= C1C1C1C1
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
329 +++ DSNAME = substr(dsntmp,1,(pos(' ',dsntmp)-1)) /* DSNAME */
277 +++ call PARSEMSG
84 +++ call MAINLOOP
Error running MAIL2EXE, line 329: Incorrect call to routine
READY
/*
READY
END

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This type of S0C4 error can be caused by one of the following.
1) The SSID in MAIL4NFY did not match the SSID in SSMPARMS in the INSTALL library.
2) Can also occur when multiple Notify STC's are active at the same time which causes storage overlays resulting in the s0c4.
Verify that multiple tasks are not running.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SLM Install Service – 1067 Error
content:
Article ID: D14912
Defect ID: DEF79577
Product: Licensing 2.1.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When you install and select to run SLM as a service then when you re-boot, and anytime you startup the system, the SLM service fails to start, and there is an event recorded in the event log.
If you try and manually start the service you will get a “1067” error like:
Windows could not start the Serena License Server service on Local Computer. Error 1067: The process terminated unexpectedly.
or
The SLM Server service could not be started.
A system error has occurred.
System error 1067 has occurred.
The process terminated unexpectedly.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The most likely reason for this is not having a serena.lic file, or all licenses in that file being invalid. Check the SLM.log file (SLM >= 2.3.0) or SerenaLicensesServer.log file (SLM <= 2.2.0) for context.
Example for missing license file:
15:35:05 (lmgrd) license manager: can't initialize:Cannot find license file.
Example for a license file with an invalid host ID on the SERVER line:
15:41:15 (merant) No valid hostids, exiting
15:41:15 (merant) EXITING DUE TO SIGNAL 25 Exit reason 2
15:41:20 (lmgrd) merant exited with status 25 (Invalid host)
15:41:20 (lmgrd) Please correct problem and restart daemons
Example for a license file that is either empty or where all keys are either invalid or expired:
16:49:06 (merant) No features to serve, exiting
16:49:06 (merant) EXITING DUE TO SIGNAL 27 Exit reason 4
16:49:11 (lmgrd) merant exited with status 27 (No features to serve)
16:49:11 (lmgrd) merant daemon found no features. Please correct
16:49:11 (lmgrd) license file and re-start daemons.
Once valid licenses exist in the serena.lic file, the service should launch without error. You can use the SLM GUI (serenalm) to add these keys. Using SLM 2.3.0 or newer is recommended.
If you have to use SLM 2.2.0, be sure to launch it using right-click | Run as administrator. Not doing this may result in the GUI not being able to save the serena.lic file.

[content]
DEF79577

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: Tomcat will not stay running. Console log gives: Failed upgrading SLA server schema
content:
Article ID: S143323

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade, Tomcat will start, run for a few seconds, and stop. When looking in the console.log, the following error displays:
2020-11-17T15:10:48,422 ERROR [localhost-startStop-2] [org.apache.catalina.core.ContainerBase.[Catalina].[localhost].[/commonsvc]:182] [::] -- Exception sending context initialized event to listener instance of class [org.springframework.web.context.ContextLoaderListener]
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'org.springframework.beans.factory.config.MethodInvokingFactoryBean#0' defined in class path resource [sla-context.xml]: Invocation of init method failed; nested exception is com.serena.core.runtime.CoreException: Failed upgrading SLA server schema.
at org.springframework.beans.factory.support.AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.initializeBean(AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.java:1630) ~[spring-beans-4.3.25.RELEASE.jar:4.3.25.RELEASE]
at org.springframework.beans.factory.support.AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.doCreateBean(AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.java:553) ~[spring-beans-4.3.25.RELEASE.jar:4.3.25.RELEASE]
at org.springframework.beans.factory.support.AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.createBean(AbstractAutowireCapableBeanFactory.java:481) ~[spring-beans-4.3.25.RELEASE.jar:4.3.25.RELEASE]
at org.springframework.beans.factory.support.AbstractBeanFactory$1.getObject(AbstractBeanFactory.java:312) ~[spring-beans-4.3.25.RELEASE.jar:4.3.25.RELEASE]

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
There was a problem with the upgrade. Gather the following information and open a case with support:
Collect Logs output from Configurator
The contents of the SLA_REGISTRY table
List of all columns in the SLA_ITEM table
List of all columns in the SLA_RULES table

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Some Dim Clients give error "Failed to start Dimensions server process"
content:
Article ID: S141023

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When the user tries to connect to Dimensions using the Dim Client, they receive error:
Failed to connect to the Dimensions server.
ACL4502922E Error: Cannot open connection to host xxxxxxxxxxxxx
Failed to start Dimensions server process
Event viewer shows the following errors:
DMPOOL E P1496 T1956 rpc_StartServer_srv2(000000000000006B8): Session activation failed for user xxxxxxxxx, connecting to xxxxx@DIM12 with xxxxx from xxxxxx (6)
and
DMPOOL E P1496 T1956 Application server initialization failed
Turning on trace logging, the DMAPPSRV log will show the following errors:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
ORA-12154: TNS: could not resolve the connect identifier specified
DBconnectToDBMS: Status - -1
Error: Unable to connect to database "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The system is not able to connect to the database. Check to listener.bat file (located in \Dimensions\CM\dfs\listener.dat). Verify the service, user, and dsn values. When the user logs into the Dim client, the client must be using the same connection information as found in the listener.dat.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

[content]
DEF349678

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

[content]
DEF349678

[contentbody]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

[content]
DEF257172

The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:

[DESCRIPTION]
Description

found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: Transition Report gives "[PARSING ERROR:] Incorrect rich text cell data format Parser Fatal Error at file Cell Data"
content:
Article ID: D28294
Defect ID: DEF349848
Product: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When running a transition listing report, the name of the transition will show as "[PARSING ERROR]" in the Excel document when the transition name includes an ampersand '&'.
In the case that two transitions with the same name are selected to be included, the selected names will include the state to which each transition leads. If the state name has an ampersand '&', this same error will happen.
The error appears in the cell in Excel
in the Transition column like this:
"[PARSING ERROR:] Incorrect rich text cell data format
Parser Fatal Error at file Cell Data, line 1, char 74 Message: expected entity name for reference"
How is this reproduced?
Add a transition called "Test & Verify" to your workflow and deploy. Alternatively, you can create 2 transitions with the same name and have one of those transitions lead to a state with an ampersand in the name, e.g. a state name of "Testing & Verifying".
Create an item and transition it using this transition.
C
reate and run a Transition Listing Report and select your transition that includes the "&" symbol in its name.
Run the report and export it to Excel. View the Transition column in Excel.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
As a workaround, change the name of your transition or state to "Test and Verify" instead of "Test & Verify"

DEF349848

title: Serena Knowledgebase - State Activity Trend reports cause Snapshots to contain bad xml so promote fails - ERROR -- Parser Fatal Error at file ... invalid character 0x2
content:
Article ID: D24252
Defect ID: DEF299957
Product: SBM 11.2
Fixed In: SBM 11.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you create a State Activity Trend report and then take a snapshot - this cannot be promoted - you get an error similar to below:
ERROR -- Parser Fatal Error at file C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Application Engine/mst82934/teamtrack/2bc73501-b2b4-46a9-9672-157876dac15e/instance.xml, line 1363, char 18 Message: invalid character 0x2

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is due to bad XML that was saved in the report definition in earlier version. To work around this problem, edit the report, save it again, and then promote the snapshot.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - High-bit characters generate TeamScript parser errors
content:
Article ID: S125183

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Build Number(s): All
If you try to assign a high-bit character to a string variable, the parser
will flag this as an error. For example: dim msg = "zÃ¤z" will fail. If this is
part of an error message to be sent to the browser, you can work around this by
entity encoding the high-bit characters and it will work; for example: dim msg =
"z&#228z". TeamScript does not have a problem with high-bit characters that
are read from field values at run-time and later output, it is only a problem
when the script definition is parsed for validation.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Upgrade from 6.0: When start manual task, get error "A Schema Parser Exception occurred while parsing the response at line 1:3167"
content:
Article ID: S141590

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you upgrade from Release Control 6.0 to 6.0.1 or 6.1, are using the default Manual Deployment Task process app, and choose not to upgrade to the latest version of that process app, you will not have proper options available on the form when you add a deployment task using the associated SBM plugin execution provider. Deployment unit and Request domain objects, such as syncStatus, syncMessage, and providerData are not compatible with the WSDL from the 6.0 release due to the WSDL/SOAP specification. In this situation, when you try to start a manual task, the user will get following error:
Error occurred during web service invocation:
A Schema Parser exception occurred while parsing the response at line 1:3167
Schema Parser Exception : Could not find element actionDisplay in task
Error validating schema instance

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
To update the WSDL in the version 6.0 Manual Deployment Task process app to be compatible with Release Control 6.0.1 and 6.1, do the following:
Open SBM Composer.
Open the RLC - Manual Deployment Task process app.
Select Extensions > Web Services > RLCTaskExecutionEndpoint.
Select Property Editor > General and click Reimport.
Point to the latest WSDL version of the RLCTaskExecutionEndpoint service by entering the following link:
http://RLCserver/rlc/taskExecutionService?wsdl
where RLCServer is your Release Control server host name or IP address.
Click OK.
A message should appear saying the import was successful.
Deploy the process app.
In SBM Configurator > Manage Services, restart JBoss.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Orchestrations failing to get called with "The requested resource (/OEGatewayService/oegs) is not available." and "Xml Parser Exception : unexpected type" errors
content:
Article ID: S141341

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Orchestrations failing to get called may display the following errors in the user workspace:
"The requested resource (/OEGatewayService/oegs) is not available."
and
"Xml Parser Exception : unexpected type"
There may also be the following error in the server.log:
"ERROR [main] [org.jboss.ws.core.server.ServiceEndpointDeployer] [::] -- Cannot create service endpoint
org.jboss.ws.WSException: Cannot obtain url pattern for servlet name: OEGatewayServlet"
This can be caused by a file such as the "web.xml" located in the "Program Files\Serena\SBM\Common\jboss405\server\default\deploy\OEGatewayService.war\WEB-INF" directory being corrupted or missing.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Replacing the file with a good copy of the file and restarting the Serena Common JBoss service should resolve the issue.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - No declaration found for element 'Locale' error during deployment
content:
Article ID: S141136

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When you move SBM from one machine to another and you see the following error during a deployment:
INFO -- Application Engine callback at 4/14/15 3:29 PM. INFO -- The following errors or warnings were received: ERROR -- Parser Error at file C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Application Engine/mst50586/application.xml, line 7, char 12 Message: no declaration found for element 'Locale' Failed to complete the import at 4/14/15 3:29 PM.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Open the SBM Application Repository and login. Go to the Environment where the deployment failed and check to make sure the Target Server and Runtime URL's are correct, including the BPEL and Event Manager URL's.
You will also see this error if you try to deploy from a 10.1.5 SBM install to a 10.1.3 or earlier version.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

[content]
DEF349678

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

DEF257172

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: HTTP status server 500 - Internal Server Error when trying to get OAuth2 access tokens for mailbox/SMTP server
content:
Article ID: D28280
Defect ID: DEF349753
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
In the SBM Application Administrator > Emails > Mailboxes or Mail Servers, it is necessary to acquire access tokens for O365. On token refresh, you may receive a HTTP error after logging in.
The TOMCAT.LOG will display an error like this:
2025-05-15 15:09:39,022 ERROR [ajp-nio-0.0.0.0-6009-exec-6] [org.apache.catalina.core.ContainerBase.[Catalina].[localhost].[/commonsvc].[resourcemgmt]:175] [::] -- Servlet.service() for servlet [resourcemgmt] in context with path [/commonsvc] threw exception [Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]] with root cause
org.apache.jasper.JasperException: Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]
at org.apache.jasper.compiler.DefaultErrorHandler.jspError(DefaultErrorHandler.java:37) ~[jasper.jar:10.1.34]
This is caused by an older jakarta library.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
WORK AROUND
1. Stop Tomcat server
2. Go to ~
\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib and copy 2 files:
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-3.0.1.jar
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api-3.0.0.jar
3. Copy these 2 jar files to locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\WEB-INF\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\WEB-INF\lib
4. Optionally, you can remove next files:
taglibs-standard-impl-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-jstlel-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-spec-1.2.5.jar
from the next locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\lib
5. Start Tomcat server

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Error: Root element is missing - importing a Process App into Composer after generating it from a Get from AE if any of your Process Apps contain a Reset Data Source
content:
Article ID: D24251
Defect ID: DEF299981
Product: SBM 11.2
Fixed In: SBM 11.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you create any Rest Data Source in ANY Process app and deploy and then do a Get from AE ALL process apps will now fail when importing to Composer. They all contain this Rest Data Source (including the global) and will fail with error "Root element is missing".
To reproduce:
(1) Take any Process App and add the Rest Data Source in Composer.
(2) Deploy
(3) Do an get of ANY Process App in the environment and try to import into Composer. This change to one process APP affects the GET of ALL process Apps.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
These generated Process Apps from a Get can be fixed. Please contact Support for help.

DEF299981

title: Serena Knowledgebase - After upgrading to VM 8.4.6.0 Combo Hotfix 1 or VM 8.5.0.0, Version Manager can no longer access archives in paths containing a subdirectory named ROOT
content:
Article ID: D19825
Defect ID: DEF256144
Product: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.00
Fixed In: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If the path to an archive contains a subdirectory named ROOT, attempts to access this archive from VM 8.4.6.0 Combo Hotfix 1 or VM 8.5.0.0 will fail. The error messages show that the subdirectory ROOT is treated as the beginning of the archive path.
For example, if the Project database contains the archive path \\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1
\ROOT\
File1.txt-arc then an attempt to use this file will fail with the error:
[07-22-2014 03:03:18.184] VmEntityValidateListener: addAnswerToCache=false, exception=Serena PVCS Version Manager could not read the information stored in the archive "\\File1.txt-arc". Please contact your Serena administrator for further assistance.
Error: /Project1/ROOT/File1.txt:
/Project1/ROOT/File1.txt
Serena PVCS Version Manager could not read the information stored in the archive "\\File1.txt-arc". Please contact your Serena administrator for further assistance.
You can get similar error messages trying to access a Configuration File or Access Control Database that is located in a path containing a subdirectory named "ROOT".

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
As a workaround, change the paths in the VM Project Database that contain the subdirectory "ROOT" such that this directory is referenced using a different case variant, like "
Root
" or "
root
". If the file system and/or the File Server are configured for case-insensitive file access, making this change will permit the affected clients to use the files in their original location, without the need to rename the "ROOT" subdirectories.
To update archive locations for individual files, you can execute the command:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"
PathToProjectDatabase
" -id"
UserID
:
Password
" -a"
ArchivePath
" "
EntityPathToFile
"
For the error message in the example above, the following command would solve the problem:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB" -id"Admin" -a"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1\
Root
\File1.txt-arc" "/Project1/
ROOT
/File1.txt"
Note that only the case of the archive directory was changed, not the case of the name of the subproject containing the file (the entity path).
To change the default location on the project itself, so newly added files won't run into problems either, use the same command on the project instead of the file:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB" -id"Admin" -a"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1\
Root
" "/Project1/
ROOT
"

[content]
DEF256144

[contentbody]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim CM 2009 R2: UNIX - Web Client allows files to be renamed to contain double quotes
content:
Article ID: D12191
Defect ID: DEF199286
Product: Dimensions CM 2009 R2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.1 Dimensions CM 12.2 Release Manager 2.0 Release Manager 2.1 Release Vault 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Using the Web Client you can rename an item filename so that it includes a double quote. This then causes problems performing a Get of the item, resulting in the following error: -
java.io.FileNotFoundException: C:\test".c (The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect)
The problem seems to occur using UNIX Dimensions servers only. This cannot be reproduced on Windows as you get the following message when you attempt to rename the file to include the double quote: -
Set Filename - COR0005885E Error: Filename tes"t1.dat contains one or more of the following invalid characters: "*|?<>:
