title: Serena Knowledgebase - Orchestration fails when WSDL contains multiple parts (xsds)
content:
Article ID: D12208
Defect ID: DEF199355
Product: SBM 2009 R4
Found In: Service Manager 1.0
Fixed In: SBM 2009 R4.01 Service Manager 1.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you have an orchestration that calls a webservice for which the WSDL contains multiple parts (i.e. xsd's) then when this web service call is reached in the orchestration the orchestration will fail with an errors similar to the following:
(1) The orchestration actually fails with "Orchestration workflow failed. Contact SBM administrator ....".
(2) The server log contains an error starting with similar to the following:
"00000:OPENARMWorkflow ERROR 2011-03-02 13:54:20,308 org.jbpm.graph.def.GraphElement -- action threw exception: null
java.lang.NullPointerException"
(3) The Common Log will show the following DEBUG error before the orchestration fails as per (1)
WSDLException: faultCode=OTHER_ERROR: Unable to resolve imported document at 'resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl'.: This file was not found: file:/C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Common/jboss405/bin/resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl: java.io.FileNotFoundException: This file was not found: file:/C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Common/jboss405/bin/resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9:21043-WS_ROOT_HAS_ITEM Error: The Work Set root contains an Item named %s
content:
Article ID: S124525

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
KM-Dim9:21043-WS_ROOT_HAS_ITEM Error: The Work Set root contains an Item named %s

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Please gather any applicable information from the list below and contact Dimensions Support for help with this error message.
What you need to troubleshoot:
A screen shot of the full error message. Note: The error message text in your error may vary slightly from this article.
The version and patch level of:
The Dimensions server
The Dimensions server Operating System
The Dimensions client
The Dimensions client Operating System
The Browser (if the error occurred using the Admin Console or Web Client)
The Java in use (if the error occurred using the Admin Console or Web Client)
The IDE used, if applicable
The Database platform and version being used
The exact list of steps to recreate the error message
A detailed control plan report
Any additional details or background information related to this problem
Additional information that may be helpful:
A detailed control plan report
Event logs (Windows OS only)
/var/adm/messages file (Unix Only)
Oracle alert log
Please answer these questions about the problem:
Is the problem reproducible every time? If not, can you determine any factors that cause the error message (ie: the CPU is maxed out when the error occurs)?
Does the problem occur on all machines? If not, is there anything consistent about the machines it does occur on (ie: only windows XP machines)?
Does the problem occur on for all users? If not, is there anything consistent about the users who get the error (ie: is it only users with the developer role)?
How to contact Dimensions Support:
Please use the link below for information about contacting Dimensions Support:
http://support.serena.com/kb/articles/dimensions/kb70712.asp

title: Serena Knowledgebase - VM: PCLI - The project root could not be loaded: "ProjectDBPath" -or- The project root could not be loaded: ""
content:
Article ID: S120118

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When running a PCLI command the following message is returned:
The project root could not be loaded: "
ProjectDBPath
".

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
If the error contains a non-empty
ProjectDBPath
then this path is invalid or the Project Database Root is not recognized.
First check the
ProjectDBPath
by opening the Project Database in the GUI and taking note of the path that is specified. This is the path that needs to be passed to the PCLI command, either by using the option
-pr"
ProjectDBPath
"
or by using the environment variable
PCLI_PR
and assigning the value "
ProjectDBPath
" to it.
If the Project Database is using Windows-style paths and PCLI is running on Linux/UNIX, replace all backslash characters (\) with forward slash ones (/) to avoid the shell for treating them as escape characters and mangling the path before it gets to PCLI.
If the Version Manager File Server is used to access your project databases, and other users of that File Server can access the identical
ProjectDBPath
without getting this error, then PCLI may lack the information it needs to locate the File Server. The process that Version Manager uses to locate the File Server is documented in KB doc
S134330
.
Possible culprits that prevent Version Manager from finding the File Server:
Missing File Server definition in
servers.ini
file or not having this file at all.
Missing reference to servers.ini in the file
ISLV.INI
(Windows) or
.islvrc
(Linux/UNIX) or not having this file at all.
Note that on Linux/UNIX every user needs to have his or her own copy of .islvrc in their home directory.
See KB doc
S138903
to learn where Version Manager is looking for the ISLV.INI / .islvrc file.
Users of VM 8.4.4 or older running in setuid mode on Linux/UNIX may also run into a permissions problem. See KB doc
D12816
for more information.
If
ProjectDBPath
in the error message shows up empty, as in:
The project root could not be loaded: "".
then one of the following as happened:
The option
-pr
was not specified and no
PCLI_PR
variable was defined.
The option
-pr
was specified as
─pr
or
—pr
meaning the option does not start with a regular DASH (
"-"
, a.k.a. the minus character) but one of the following:
An EN-DASH
"─"
(Unicode U+2013 / ASCII code 150 or 0x96 in CodePage 1250 / ASCII code 196 or 0xC4 in CodePage 1252)
An EM-DASH "—" (Unicode U+2014 / ASCII code 151 or 0x97 in CodePage 1250)
Although these characters look like a dash they are fundamentally different and not recognized by command line utilities as option lead-in characters.
This can happen when commands are copied from Microsoft Office or Outlook documents, when those tools have been configured to replace certain characters with nicer looking equivalents (dashes, smart quotes, etc.). While the replacement characters may be more pleasing to the eye, they are not functionally equivalent and will cause failures when used from command line tools like PCLI.

[content]
When running a PCLI command the following message is returned:
The project root could not be loaded: "
ProjectDBPath
".

[contentbody]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SRA: Agent installation fails on linux when AgentOwner is not root
content:
Article ID: S141159

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Silent install of agent on Linux fails with no error code when the options file contains a non-root user such as the following:
-V AgentOwner="serena"
The installLocation and the IS_DESTINATION were both set to /opt/serena/Release_Automation_Agent, however the "serena" folder did not yet exist.
The log files in the /tmp folder contained the runWrapped log and this indicated an error attempting to invoke the java.
After the installer failed, the Release_Automation_Agent folder and subfolders existed, but the core folder was empty.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The installation script was being run by user root. Therefore, at the start of the installation the folder serena is created underneath of opt. The serena folder is owned by root and the only user with permissions on serena was root.
The Release_Automation_Agent folder was also created. The installer set this to be owned by serena and the permissions were set appropriately on this folder. Because the _jvm folder (underneath Release_Automation_Agent) was still under the path of "serena", the user serena could not access this folder to start the jvm.
To resolve this problem, prior to running the installation, create the serena folder and perform a chmod 755 on this folder. Then invoke the silent installer.

[content]
Silent install of agent on Linux fails with no error code when the options file contains a non-root user such as the following:

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Can Tracker point to the PVCSTRK.INI file in a different location than the system root?
content:
Article ID: S119328

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Setting Tracker and Notify to point to the initialization file (pvcstrk.ini) in a different location than the system root
On certain scenarios, Tracker users do not have permissions on the system root directory (C:\Winnt or C:\Windows) of the machines due to their company's security policy.

Tracker points to the initialization file PVCSTRK.INI file that is created in the system root of the machine while Tracker is installed. This file contains personal settings for Tracker, Tracker Admin, and Notify. Tracker and Notify applications read these settings when they are started.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The initialization file 'pvcstrk.ini' can be copied
from the system root directory to a different location where the Tracker users
have full control permission and, the Tracker, Notify applications can be made
to point to the file in the new location.
Following are the steps to copy the 'pvcstrk.ini' file
from the system root directory to a different location and set Tracker GUI,
Tracker Admin and Notify to point to the file in the new location:
1.
Copy the 'pvcstrk.ini' file from C:\Winnt to another drive (Example: D:\PVCS\newpvcstrk.ini.)
2.
Navigate to Start | Programs | PVCS Tracker | PVCS Tracker.
3.
Right click and select 'Properties'.
4.
The 'Target' option box will have the location of the Tracker executable file
(Example: 'D:\PVCS\Tracker 7.1\Tracker\nt\pvcstkn.exe')
5.
Add the path of the 'newpvcstrk.ini' file to the end of the Tracker executable
path with a '/I' variable (Example: The 'Target' option should have a value like
'D:\PVCS\Tracker 7.1\Tracker\nt\pvcstkn.exe'/I D:\PVCS\newpvcstrk.ini.).
This
will enable the Tracker GUI read the 'newpvcstrk.ini' file.
Repeat
the same steps selecting Tracker Admin in step 2 (Navigate to Start | Programs |
PVCS Tracker | PVCS Tracker Admin). This will enable Tracker Admin read the
'newpvcstrk.ini' file when it is opened.
Also,
follow the same steps selecting PVCS Notify in step 2 (Navigate to Start |
Programs | PVCS Tracker | PVCS Notify). This will enable Notify read the
'newpvcstrk.ini' file when it is opened.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: HTTP status server 500 - Internal Server Error when trying to get OAuth2 access tokens for mailbox/SMTP server
content:
Article ID: D28280
Defect ID: DEF349753
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
In the SBM Application Administrator > Emails > Mailboxes or Mail Servers, it is necessary to acquire access tokens for O365. On token refresh, you may receive a HTTP error after logging in.
The TOMCAT.LOG will display an error like this:
2025-05-15 15:09:39,022 ERROR [ajp-nio-0.0.0.0-6009-exec-6] [org.apache.catalina.core.ContainerBase.[Catalina].[localhost].[/commonsvc].[resourcemgmt]:175] [::] -- Servlet.service() for servlet [resourcemgmt] in context with path [/commonsvc] threw exception [Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]] with root cause
org.apache.jasper.JasperException: Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]
at org.apache.jasper.compiler.DefaultErrorHandler.jspError(DefaultErrorHandler.java:37) ~[jasper.jar:10.1.34]
This is caused by an older jakarta library.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
WORK AROUND
1. Stop Tomcat server
2. Go to ~
\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib and copy 2 files:
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-3.0.1.jar
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api-3.0.0.jar
3. Copy these 2 jar files to locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\WEB-INF\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\WEB-INF\lib
4. Optionally, you can remove next files:
taglibs-standard-impl-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-jstlel-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-spec-1.2.5.jar
from the next locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\lib
5. Start Tomcat server

[content]
DEF349753

org.apache.jasper.JasperException: Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]
at org.apache.jasper.compiler.DefaultErrorHandler.jspError(DefaultErrorHandler.java:37) ~[jasper.jar:10.1.34]
This is caused by an older jakarta library.
Resolution
WORK AROUND
1. Stop Tomcat server
2. Go to ~
\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib and copy 2 files:
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-3.0.1.jar
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api-3.0.0.jar
3. Copy these 2 jar files to locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\WEB-INF\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\WEB-INF\lib
4. Optionally, you can remove next files:
taglibs-standard-impl-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-jstlel-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-spec-1.2.5.jar
from the next locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\lib
5. Start Tomcat server

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: When setting up OAuth2 for Mail Server, refresh the Access Token gives error "Unknown error occurred. Please contact your Administrator"
content:
Article ID: D27911
Defect ID: DEF347108
Product: SBM 11.8
Fixed In: SBM 11.8 Hotfix

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When setting up a Mail Server for Email Recorder to use OAuth2 authentication, the Access Token must be refreshed. This caused the user to be prompted for login information. The login will spin for a little bit, and then following error is displayed to the user:
"Unknown error occurred. Please contact your Administrator"
This issue is specific to servers without direct access to the internet. Instead, a proxy server is used.
The server.log with debug logging shows that the system is getting a timeout while trying to connect to login.microsoft.com

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: Request for Oauth2 token fails for endpoint with error "The error occurred while requesting tokens."
content:
Article ID: D28231
Defect ID: DEF349537
Product: SBM 12.2
Fixed In: SBM 12.2 Patch 1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
In the Application Repository, the Endpoint requires Oauth2 authentication, but the request for a token refresh fails.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Setup an endpoint with Oauth2 authentication
2. Click Refresh token and provide username and password in login popup
Expected popup:
Tokens were successfully generated.
Actual popup:
The error occurred while requesting tokens.
Also, the following warning will appear in mashupmgr.log:
mashupmgr.OAuth2 -- Unable to request OAuth 2 tokens: callback request has no code parameter because of 'null'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This will happen if the IAM service returns the locale attribute in the auth code callback url, for example:
http://SERVER:8085/mashupmgr/hapi/oauth2?
code
=vpS...61&
locale=en
&state=STATE&userState=AuthenticatedNotRegistered

[content]
DEF349537

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM NS server tries to contact OAUTH2 auth server the tokens are present and authentication is none
content:
Article ID: D28276
Defect ID: DEF349600
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The notification server logs may indicate that it is creating and sending messages out, but the user may never receive the message.
When we put the NS logging in debug mode, we found that the NS was trying to authenticate to Outlook 365 with an expired access token. This notification server had previously been setup to use Outlook 365. The administrator then opted to setup an SMTP relay server instead of using the Outlook 365 SMTP which caused the issue because the SMTP settings for OAuth2 were not cleared prior to setting authentication to "None".
When the NS failed to get the token, it threw an error and then proceeded mark the message as sent and delete it from the messages table.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
As a work around, login to the Application Administrator > Notifications > System Settings and update t
he SMTP server settings. Temporarily set authentication to OAuth2 and delete the tokens and setup from the setup fields. Once the OAuth2 settings are cleared, set your authentication back to none and the issue will be resolved.

[content]
DEF349600

content:
Article ID: S140976

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When attempting to access the VM I-Net home page, the user is receiving this error:
HTTP Status 500 - Servlet execution threw an exception
type Exception report
message Servlet execution threw an exception
description The server encountered an internal error that prevented it from fulfilling this request.
exception
javax.servlet.ServletException: Servlet execution threw an exception
org.apache.tomcat.websocket.server.WsFilter.doFilter(WsFilter.java:52)
root cause
java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError: C:\Program Files (x86)\Serena\vm\common\bin\win32\pvcsjvms.dll: The specified procedure could not be found
java.lang.ClassLoader$NativeLibrary.load(Native Method)
java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary1(ClassLoader.java:1965)
java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary0(ClassLoader.java:1890)
java.lang.ClassLoader.loadLibrary(ClassLoader.java:1880)
java.lang.Runtime.loadLibrary0(Runtime.java:849)
java.lang.System.loadLibrary(System.java:1088)
pvcs.vm.vms.Init.load(Init.java:53)
pvcs.vm.vms.Init.loadOrExit(Init.java:99)
pvcs.vm.vms.ArchiveSearch.<clinit>(ArchiveSearch.java:98)
pvcs.vm.services.file.VmSvcArchiveFile.<init>(VmSvcArchiveFile.java:159)
pvcs.vm.services.file.VmServicesFile.oneTimeInitialization(VmServicesFile.java:81)
pvcs.vm.services.file.VmServicesFile.init(VmServicesFile.java:68)
pvcs.system.PvcsConnection.addInterface(PvcsConnection.java:303)
pvcs.system.PvcsSystem.addInterface(PvcsSystem.java:1151)
pvcs.system.PvcsConnection.addInterfacesFromFile(PvcsConnection.java:357)
pvcs.system.PvcsSystem.loadSystem(PvcsSystem.java:1222)
pvcs.system.PvcsSystem.getSystem(PvcsSystem.java:370)
pvcs.suite.SuiteSystem.initSuiteSystem(SuiteSystem.java:1739)
pvcs.suite.SuiteSystem.<init>(SuiteSystem.java:133)
pvcs.suite.SuiteSystem.createSuiteSystem(SuiteSystem.java:171)
pvcs.suite.SuiteSystem.getSuiteSystem(SuiteSystem.java:157)
pvcs.vm.servlet.VmServletViewer.dependentInit(VmServletViewer.java:1126)
pvcs.vm.servlet.VmServletViewer.delayedInit(VmServletViewer.java:1104)
pvcs.vm.servlet.VmServletViewer.service(VmServletViewer.java:406)
javax.servlet.http.HttpServlet.service(HttpServlet.java:727)
org.apache.tomcat.websocket.server.WsFilter.doFilter(WsFilter.java:52)

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This error can occur when you have Version Manager and is installed along side with our Serena Dimensions Product and Dimensions was installed last.
More so this occurs when the Envirtonment Variable Path to Dimensions DLLs are placed ahead of Version Manager's. For instance the cause of this error would look somewhat like this:
PATH=
C:\Program Files\Serena\Dimensions 14.1\CM\prog
;C:\Program Files (x86)\Serena\vm\win32\bin;C:\Program Files (x86)\Serena\vm\common\bin\win32;...
To remedy the situation you would need to rearrange the values of the Path variable as so, by moving the Dimensions value after Version Manager's:
PATH=C:\Program Files (x86)\Serena\vm\win32\bin;C:\Program Files (x86)\Serena\vm\common\bin\win32;
C:\Program Files\Serena\Dimensions 14.1\CM\prog
;...

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Problem:   VMINET : Left hand pane (containing buttons) shows a 500 Internal Server error
content:
Article ID: S122580

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Microsoft Proxy Server 2.0 has a defect that will not pass through a Java ASP (which the pages in VM I-Net are made of), and so the user may see a 500 internal server error in place of the left hand pane.
Microsoft reports that Proxy Server 2.0 Service Pack 1 will fix this error. See URL below to obtain this service pack.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
From Microsoft KnowledgeBase Article ID: Q183283
Internet Explorer Through Proxy to IIS May Stop on Page with Java or ASP Scripts
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The information in this article applies to:
Microsoft Internet Information Server version 4.0
Microsoft Proxy Server version 2.0
Microsoft Internet Explorer version 4.01 for Windows 95
Microsoft Internet Explorer version 4.01 for Windows NT 4.0
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SYMPTOMS
When Internet Explorer version 4.01 connects through Microsoft Proxy Server version 2.0 to a Microsoft Internet Information Server (IIS) 4.0 computer, Internet Explorer may hang when you refresh a page that contains Java or ASP scripts. The page will be incomplete, and the images may fail to download.
NOTE: This problem will not occur if you use IIS version 3.0, or if you use Microsoft Internet Explorer version 3.0 to access an IIS 4.0 computer.
CAUSE
A content-length header may be missing from the IIS 4.0 HTTP response.
RESOLUTION
To resolve this problem, obtain the Microsoft Proxy Server 2.0 Service Pack 1. For additional information on Service Pack 1, please reference the following Microsoft Knowledge Base article:
Q238375
Proxy Server 2.0 Service Pack 1: List of Fixes
WORKAROUND
To work around this problem, contact the administrator of the Web site, and ask him or her to contact Microsoft Product Support Services to obtain and install the IIS fix discussed below.
STATUS
Microsoft has confirmed this to be a problem in the Microsoft products listed at the beginning of this article.
MORE INFORMATION
There are two fixes available for this problem, one for IIS 4.0 and one for Proxy Server 2.0. The IIS fix can be applied to any IIS 4.0 Web server that users connect to using Internet Explorer version 4.x.
Because this problem occurs more frequently when Microsoft Internet Explorer 4.x connects through Microsoft Proxy Server to an IIS 4.0 Web site, a fix has been created for Microsoft Proxy Server 2.0. This version of the fix can be installed on the Proxy Server computer and is the preferred solution because it will correct the problem for connections to ALL IIS 4.0 web sites.
Additional query words:
Keywords : kbfaq iis4faq
Version : WINDOWS:4.01; winnt:2.0,4.0
Platform : WINDOWS winnt
Issue type : kbbug

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Server Application Unavailable error trying to access the PE web page
content:
Article ID: S130414

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
"ISSUE:
Server Application Unavailable error trying to access the PE web
page
TROUBLESHOOTING STEPS:
Check permissions on system temp file.
1) Go to
desktop, right click on My Computer
2) Choose properties
3) Go to Advanced
Tab/Environmental Variables
4) Under System Variables, find the values of
TEMP and TMP.
5) Go to that directory, and make sure that the ASPNET user
has Full Control on it.
Get Regmon and Filemon to check the permissions:
<
http://www.sysinternals.com/ntw2k/utilities.shtml
>
The
problem is going to be with the aspnet process or user, so filter on ""aspnet""
1. kill the aspnet.exe process
2. restart IIS
3. start filemon with
the ""aspnet"" filter
4. hit PE again
After getting the log,
check explicit permissions for the aspnet_wp account on:
%windir%\temp(ie,
""c:\winnt\temp"")
%windir%\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v1.1.4322\Temporary
ASP.NET Files
The account should have full permissions on both
folders.
Try deleting all the files in the Temporary ASP.NET Files
directory(%windir%\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v1.0.4322\Temporary ASP.NET
Files)
Then restarting IIS and hitting the PE page."

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Installing the Mariner Report Server component gives error "HTTP status 401: Unauthorized"
content:
Article ID: S135680

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Installing the Mariner Report Server component gives the following error:
Skipped creating root folder /PESReports:
--- The request failed with HTTP status 401: Unauthorized.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
In IIS make sure the Reports and ReportServer virtual directories allow anonymous access.
After making this change you will have to run the Mariner install again.
Choose the "Modify" option.
Drop the option down on the Reporting Services option to make not available.
Finish the install.
When that's done start the install again and choose Reporting Services and finish.
See this kb doc for more information about intial setup of Report Server Virtual Directories.
http://knowledgebase.serena.com/InfoCenter/index?page=content&id=S137513

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: How to upgrade SBM and move to a new Windows server at the same time
content:
Article ID: S143014

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When you upgrade SBM, it is always important to make sure the new version is supported on the version of Windows you are running. For example if you have been running on a Windows 2008 Server for a long time and you want to upgrade SBM to the newest version, Windows 2008 Server is no longer supported. In this scenario, you can decide to upgrade your server to a newer version of Windows, but most customers choose to get a newer, faster server (physical or virtual) with more memory. This document will focus on the high level steps needed to upgrade SBM and also move SBM to a new server.
Tip:
You can check the version of Windows and SQL/Oracle that are supported with your version of SBM by looking at the Platform Matrix document. These can be found on the
Product Availability and Support Schedule
page. After you search for the product and version number, click the + to drill into the details. Here you will find the link to the Supported Platform List.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
NOTE:
These instructions assume that you have 1 DEV, 1 QA, and 1 PROD server where all of the components are installed. If you want to setup a load balance or clustered servers, the steps will be slightly different. This guide can still be used at an overall guide. If you have additional questions about your configuration, contact the support team.
License Manager Considerations:
Newer versions of SBM will only run with newer versions of the License Manager. Check the Platform Matrix to verify you have the correct version of License Manager installed. If your existing version of License Manager is too old for the new version of SBM, upgrade your existing installation of License Manager
before continuing
. License Manager is backwards compatible and will work normally for your existing SBM environments.
IMPORTANT:
Please make sure that you can rehost all of your SBM licenses before upgrading License Manager.
As of SBM 12 Patch 1, we are using the new AutoPass license server. This new license server will allow for a 60 days from the time that you install SBM 12 as an eval period. It is recommend that you install AutoPass ahead of your Go Live date and rehost/transfer your licenses to AutoPass. This will not affect the currently active License Manager as you will not be using AutoPass until you complete your upgrade of SBM to version 12 patch 1.
Database Considerations:
Be sure to check the Platform Matrix document (see TIP in the description). You may need to upgrade your database to a newer versions of SQL or Oracle to support the new version of SBM. If you also need to upgrade the database (or if you just want to move it to a new server), have your DBA make a backup of the existing database and move it to the new server now.
TIP:
Rather than moving your existing QA/Test database to a new server, take a backup of your PROD database and restore this as your new QA copy. This will allows you to see exactly how long the SBM upgrade will take and will give you a fresh copy of "good" data to use in your test system.
TIP:
If you have been experiencing problems with your Repository, or if you just want to clean it out, now is a good time to do so. Ask your DBA to create a new database that can used for the Repository in the new version. This should be a an empty database setup as described in the Installation and Configuration Guide, chapter 2, in the section called Preparing Your Database.
Custom Template and View Considerations:
If you have created custom report templates or email/notification templates, we recommend that you launch the SBM System Administrator on the current server, and go to File > Put Files Into Database. This will ensure that the latest copies of your templates are moved to the database and will automatically be available on the new server.
If you have any custom View Definitions, these are NOT stored in the database and will need to be manually copied to the new server. By default, these are stor

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  TT2Dim:  SQL-1843(01D1BD88) ORA-01843: not a valid month  occurs in log file
content:
Article ID: D818
Defect ID: DEF155414
Product: Dimensions CM

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The error of SQL-1843(01D1BD88) ORA-01843: not a valid month occurs in log file when using Dimensions 10.1.3.x and TT 6.6.1.x with Oracle.
The problem occurs when the NLS_LANG on the client (or server running the Connector) is not setup to use AMERICAN.
The error ocurrs due to the fact that we are using the English month name (JAN, FEB, MAR, APR, ...) in the integration ; and for some reason, if the NLS_LANG is not set to English or American, the month name could be different (for instance, JUN, ... in Italian).

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The current workaround is to:
Add NLS_DATE_LANGUAGE key in the Oracle settings in the registry under the Key_Dimensions:
Parameter Name= NLS_DATE_LANGUAGE
Parameter Value= AMERICAN
Note: If using multiple Oracle Homes, this variable may also need to be set at the Home0 key.
This issue has been forwarded to R&D for correction,
DEF155414.

[content]
DEF155414

[contentbody]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - MCLG: Upload Failed, the file selected was an invalid type when uploading a file in contribution. Exception occurred:JSP.as_propsheet_general.jsp on properties screen of asset.
content:
Article ID: D6546
Defect ID: DEF67604
Product: Collage 4.5.01

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
MCLG: Upload Failed, the file selected was an invalid type when uploading a file in contribution. Exception occurred:JSP.as_propsheet_general.jsp on properties screen of asset.
Type
Exception occurred:JSP.as_propsheet_general.jsp
URL: /servlet/as_propsheet_general.jsp TH(0) version(-5) assetId(154) allowdeleted()
Stacktrace
java.lang.NullPointerException

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Try this resolution first since it's the easiest.
Go to the Project | Asset Type setup screen and look at all the Mimetypes associated with the file extension this file uses. For example if you have multiple mimetypes that use pdf extension edit each one of them and make sure they have an associated asset type.
You're looking for one or more that have no asset type specified and a red x next to them indicating a required field isn't filled in. There is a bug that allows you to delete an asset type without the corresponding mimetype to also get deleted which leaves you in this state.
Type some Dummy associated asset type name for this mimetype but don't use the same Asset Type as an existing System Asset type or you will break that asset type.
Click OK and look for any other mimetypes with this extension that don't have an associated asset type and repeat these steps.
For versions of Collage after 5.1.2 you may have to follow these steps.
Go to the asset type screen and create an empty dummy asset type, maybe call it Junk
Go to the mime type window and look at the mimetype for the offending mimetype with no asset type assigned.
Go to the System | Setup | Sessions & Servers screen and click the SQL button on the toolbar.
Run the following sql statement. The example below text/rss is the mimetype that the user deleted the corresponding asset type for.
update assettypes set basemimetype='text/rss' where assettype='Junk'
After you run this you have to restart Collage for the change to take affect. This is because the asset types are read when collage starts.
Restart Collage and open the properties of one of the offending assets and make sure it works.

[content]
DEF67604

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  TT Dimensions Connector:  Could not set value <value> for field DIMENSIONS_PROJ occurs in the log file
content:
Article ID: S132751

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
TeamTrack Dimensions Connector: Could not set value <value>
for field DIMENSIONS_PROJ occurs in the log file

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
For Dimensions 10.1.x:
Could not set value <value> for field DIMENSIONS_PROJ.
Failed.
This error is caused by the TeamTrack field not having the value mapped
and/or the value has been disabled. When using the Sync Projects button in the
Connector, the Dimensions Projects will be copied over to the field to which you
have mapped. These values will be disabled in TeamTrack until you enable
them within TeamTrack Admin.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - What problems can occur when you run audit against an out-of-date Impact Analysis file?
content:
Article ID: S100064

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
What kind of problems can you expect to encounter when you run audit against
an Impact Analysis file (Flat or DB2) that is out of date?

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The purpose of the Impact Analysis file is to maintain
relationship information for Change Man components (for example, SRC to CPY, CPY
to CPY, LOD to LOD). It is important that this relationship information be
current, since the audit process uses the data during out-of-synch processing.
Any synch errors that are based on component relationships are suspect if your
Impact Analysis data is out-of-date. Some specific synch errors that can be
impacted are SYNCH5, SYNCH8, SYNCH15, and SYNCH16.
Some potential audit problems you can encounter are:
- SYNCH5 errors are missed if the Impact Analysis data does not contain a
valid SRC to CPY
relationship. If the CPY component is changed in a
package, audit will not know to flag a
SYNCH5, indicating that the
SRC program in baseline needs to be recompiled with the new
copybook.
- SYNCH8 errors can be missed if the Impact Analysis data does not contain a
valid LOD to
LOD relationship. If a called load module in staging has
an SSI that is more current than the
calling load module in baseline,
you need to relink the baseline version of the calling load
module.
These errors could be missed.
- SYNCH15 errors are missed if the Impact Analysis data does not contain a
valid SRC to
CPY relationship. If the contents of the CPY component
in staging, promotion, or baseline
has been changed since the source
was compiled, a SYNCH15 should be returned from
audit indicating that
the SRC component needs to be restaged. These errors could be
missed.
- SYNCH16 errors are be reported on SRC to CPY and CPY to CPY relationships
that are
known to Impact Analysis, but are no longer valid
relationships. In the event that the Impact
Analysis data contains a
relationship that has been subsequently broken, an invalid
SYNCH16
could be returned from audit.
Audit results become suspect when it is run on out-of-date Impact Analysis
data. Using the flat file makes it even more difficult because there is no
incremental update as there is for the DB2 Impact Analysis table.

[content]
What kind of problems can you expect to encounter when you run audit against
an Impact Analysis file (Flat or DB2) that is out of date?

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim12: When doing a GET of a source file to a pds, an error occurs of Get Item - MDHFSY0004583E target record length too small (124)->(80)
content:
Article ID: S140682

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When doing a GET of a source file to a pds, an error occurs as follows:
FI "QLARIUS:TEST124 SRC-146407854X29529X11.A-SRC;1" /USER_FILENAME="L_S001::DMSYS.DEMO.SRC@(TEST124@)" /NOEXPAND /OVERWRITE
Get Item - MDHFSY0004583E target record length too small (124)->(80)
MDHFSY0004621I Dataset incompatable with selected item
MDHNCL4501190E Error: DMSYS.DEMO.SRC(TEST124): I/O error

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This error occurs because the item has been created from a member of a pds defined with parameter "record length" (LRECL) set to 124 and in the FI command the target pds is defined with a record length of 80. Then as Dimensions has recorded that the item has been created from a pds with LRECL=124 then Dimensions expects that the target pds of the FI command has the same value for LRECL.
2 solutions to get FI command working :
a) specify a pds with LRECL=124 in the FI command
b) or if its mandatory to do the FI command with a target pds with LRECL=80 then delete the item and recreate it from a pds with LRECL=80

[content]
When doing a GET of a source file to a pds, an error occurs as follows:

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  When saving a target file within the Merge Compare Utility, an error "Target file has not been specified or is invalid. Select the different file as the target from configuration dialog" occurs.
content:
Article ID: S132874

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When saving a target file within the Merge Compare Utility, a following error
presents:
Target file has not been specified or is invalid.
Select the different file as the target from configuration
dialog.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
For Dimensions 10.1.x and 9.1.x:
When attempting to merge an item with a workfile on the local workstation,
user will need to attempt to Compare the items for any conflicts.
When performing the compare, resolving the conflicts, and then when
attempting to save the changes to a target file, an error presents:
Target file has not been specified or is invalid.
Select the different file as the target from configuration dialog.
The error message presents because a target file has not been specified
within the configuration of the Merge Utility.
Within the Merge Utility, user needs to open View/Configuration
and specify Target filename and location for the merge utility to work.
Once done, user can perform compare and save new target file
accordingly.

Description
When saving a target file within the Merge Compare Utility, a following error
presents:
Target file has not been specified or is invalid.
Select the different file as the target from configuration
dialog.
Resolution
For Dimensions 10.1.x and 9.1.x:
When attempting to merge an item with a workfile on the local workstation,
user will need to attempt to Compare the items for any conflicts.
When performing the compare, resolving the conflicts, and then when
attempting to save the changes to a target file, an error presents:
Target file has not been specified or is invalid.
Select the different file as the target from configuration dialog.
The error message presents because a target file has not been specified
within the configuration of the Merge Utility.
Within the Merge Utility, user needs to open View/Configuration
and specify Target filename and location for the merge utility to work.
Once done, user can perform compare and save new target file
accordingly.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: Running a modscript fails with error: ERR #34 "Unavailable" and "Unknown Exception"
content:
Article ID: D28112
Defect ID: DEF349290
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2 Patch 1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When trying to run a modscript via web service, Shell.Context() gives an error such as the following in the Event Viewer:
Error occurred in file:
'D:\bld\BM_MAINLINE\BM_MAINLINE-AEX64\Application_Engine\software\teamtrack\source\Process\SLError.cpp', line 310.
User: Id 8, 'Administrator'
RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "scriptIncludeParentSubmit" (id=9)
(called from runmodscript-SOAP context)
ModScript Error -- ERR #34
Line -1> { Unavailable }
Error: "Unknown Exception"
This happens if the modscript is executed by SBM Web Services via:
Run from an orchestration in modscript context.
Run as a scheduled task.
A transition with a modscript action is triggered via SBM Web Services.
Example of a problem modscript:
// Test value of
Shell.Context() cannot be used directly in comparison operator.
if (Shell.Context() == "runmodscript-SOAP") {
var test=Shell.ScriptName();
Ext.LogErrorMsg( "Testing Logging ");
}

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution

DEF349290

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Unknown object property  --  ERR #8 when using Keys or Items method of the Scripting.Dictionary object
content:
Article ID: S138684 [DESCRIPTION] Description When using the Dictionary.Scripting object, the AE returns the following error: RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "SampleTwo" (id=28) (called from pre-transition context) Unknown object property -- ERR #8 Line 25> Next The sample code used follows: dim d,a,i,s set d=Server.CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d.Keys s = s & a & "
" next set d=nothing [RESOLUTION] Resolution AppScript does not support the use of the Items and Keys methods. Instead, use For Each on the dictionary object to return the key for each item. The value can be referenced using the standard dictionary object Item property. See sample code. dim d,a,s set d=CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d s = s & d.Item(a) & chr(13) & chr(10) next Call Shell.Item.SetFieldValue("DESCRIPTION",s) set d=nothing [content] When using the Dictionary.Scripting object, the AE returns the following error: RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "SampleTwo" (id=28) (called from pre-transition context) Unknown object property -- ERR #8 Line 25> Next The sample code used follows: dim d,a,i,s set d=Server.CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d.Keys s = s & a & "

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Example Script for AppRecord object in Mashup/Team Script Reference is faulty
content:
Article ID: S134884

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The example script on the Page 60 of Mashup Script Reference ,in the AppRecord section which begins in page 59, is faulty.
Its execution will produce the following error message :
"
RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "Aux" (id=24)
(called from post-transition context)
Wrong number of arguments -- ERR #7
Line 4> Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
"
This is because the tableId variable in line 3 and 4 of the script has not been declared .
This is the script as it appears in the Mashup Script Reference.
"
Dim myRecord, myLong
REM Create a Contact record
tableId = Ext.TableId("TS_CONTACTS")
Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
myRecord.SetName("First Middle LastName")
myLong = myRecord.Add()
If myLong = 0 Then
Call Ext.LogErrorMsg("Error adding Contact.")
End If
"
The script should look as follows :
"
Dim myRecord, myLong ,
tableId
REM Create a Contact record
tableId = Ext.TableId("TS_CONTACTS")
Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
myRecord.SetName("First Middle LastName")
myLong = myRecord.Add()
If myLong = 0 Then
Call Ext.LogErrorMsg("Error adding Contact.")
End If
"
This error has been carried over from the Teamscript Reference and it needs correcting there as well.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Troubleshooting: Notification using Run Script does not kick off the AppScript (SBM 10.1+)
content:
Article ID: S139161

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
This document is designed to help you to troubleshoot notifications that run an AppScript. If the AppScript appears to fail or does not run at all, use these steps to narrow down the problem.
NOTE:
The notification engine for SBM was changed in the 10.1 release. This document will focus on the SBM 10.1 release.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Step 1: Check the ns.log file
Check the ns.log file (by default in, C:\Program Files\Serena\SBM\Common\jboss405\server\default\work\jboss.web\localhost\notificationsrv\log) for errors. Below are 2 examples of common errors you will find in the log.
Example A:
In this example, the error is "An error occurred while sending an event", but it does not tell you what the error is. The trick in this situation are the lines BEFORE the notification. Here you will see "Invalid User ID". This is the user ID that SBM will use if the script needs to access data within the system. This user ID is set inside Configurator, under Mail Services, on the Notification Server tab, in the field called Web Services Authentication User. This user must also have the System permission called Connect Using the API.
ERROR 2012-09-25 13:28:18,920 [notificationsrv.ScriptEventService.Events] -- com.serena.core.runtime.LocalizedCoreException: Failed to obtain security token: Invalid User ID or Password
INFO 2012-09-25 13:28:18,920 [notificationsrv.ScriptEventService.Events] -- Script for notificationID: 137, eventID: 21 , userID: 0
ERROR 2012-09-25 13:28:18,922 [notificationsrv.EventService.Events] -- An error occurred while sending an event for the 'MyNotificationTest' notification.
INFO 2012-09-25 13:28:18,922 [notificationsrv.EventService.Events] -- Removing event 21, Tbl:ItemId 1000:7, from Events Table because there's no escalation conditions
Example B:
This is another example where the log says an error occurred, but does not give any information about the error. This is usually because there is an error in the script itself. Continue with Step 2 below for more troubleshooting ideas.
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:00 [Notifications] -- Notification 'My Notification' being sent on Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829 for all recipients
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:00 [Notifications] -- Added event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, to events table for notification 'My Notification'
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Initial send for event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, sending notification 'My Notification'
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Script for notificationID: 178, eventID: 5784 , userID: 28
ERROR 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- An error occurred while sending an event for the 'My Notification' notification.
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Removing event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, from Events Table because there's no escalation conditions
NOTE:
You can increase the amount of information that is logged by changing the Logging Level in Configurator under Mail Services on the Notification Server tab.
Step 2: Check the application engine log
In order for the notification to run a script, it must be passed to the Application Engine for processing. The Application Engine writes most of errors in the Event Viewer. Check the Event Viewer application log for any messages that happened at the time the notification ran. See below for example error messages.
Step 3: Run the script from a transition
If you still have not been able to find the problem, temporarily add a transition to your workflow that has an Action that will run the script, deploy to staging environment, and move an item through this transition. Any errors will be displayed at the top of the browser. See below for example error messages. REMEMBER: Some objects may not be valid in the context of a transition, so this test may not work in your situation.
Step 4: Simplify the script
If you still have not been able to find the problem, try simplifying the script. Maybe take out EVERY line in the script, and only include the following line:
Call Ext.L

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9: When attempting to perform a release baseline from PC Client the resulting  error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp' can be caused by a white space character preceding the id.
content:
Article ID: S119812

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the
id.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the id.
Run the
following script to determine character length in the id name.
sqlplus basedb/password@<sid>
SQL> select
baseline_id, length(baseline_id), base_seq_no
from baseline_history
where
baseline_id like 'build5';
SQL> select obj_id, length (obj_id),
obj_spec_uid
from bln_spec_catalogue
where obj_id like
'build5';
You will receive output similiar to below:
SQL>
select baseline_id, length(baseline_id), base_seq_no from baseline_history where
baseline_id like 'build5';
BASELINE_ID LENGTH(BASELINE_ID)
BASE_SEQ_NO
------------------------- -------------------
-----------
build 5 8 4960359
SQL> select obj_id,
length(obj_id), obj_spec_uid from bln_spec_catalogue where obj_id like
'CNARESULT';
OBJ_ID LENGTH(OBJ_ID) OBJ_SPEC_UID
--------------
------------
build 5 7 4960360
As you can see the entry in baseline
history is one character longer than the entry in bln_spec_catalogue. The
customer should run DBL 'ECS: BUILD 5' (note preceding white space chacter is
included) and baseline should be deleted.

[content]
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the
id.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - VM:  Error: Installshield scripting runtime 1607
content:
Article ID: S123171

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
VM: Error: Installshield scripting runtime 1607

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Installshield scripting runtime 1607
When installing VM 6.8.10, users may receive this type of Installshield error message when they don't have privileges to write to the system registry and/or Windows directories.
This error can appear during initial stage of installation, when Version Manager attempts create temporary files or Windows folders.
Login as a user with Administrator privileges and run the install again.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: AppScript/ModScript use incorrect locale for display of dates
content:
Article ID: D27629
Defect ID: DEF342636
Product: SBM 11.7.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2 Patch 1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
In specific use-cases, date formats in appscript or modscript may switch from your selected locale to one of the English varieties.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is an unintended consequence of a change to resolve other locale issues.
Workaround:
Create the following REG_SZ registry key:
Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\TeamShare\TeamTrack\
ScriptTimeLocale
For the value, give the locale to use. For example de-DE or en-US

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: ModScript Now() function writes wrong time after changing the OS time zone
content:
Article ID: D28113
Defect ID: DOC349292
Product: SBM 12.0

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
SBM supports a Now() function for converting SBM AppScript to SBM ModScript. The Now() function gets the current time as per the OS time zone.
However, if the OS time zone changes, the Now() function will keep getting the old time zone's information.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Restart the IIS server for the new time zone settings to be reflected in the script.

DOC349292

Description
The documentation for the return value of AppRecord Lock() Method states: "Returns true if the record is locked by another user".

title: Serena Knowledgebase - ModScript Error: "Missing clone or copy constructor for right hand side of equation" With parameters: (VarRecord)
content:
Article ID: S142385

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Example:
When calling this function from another using :
var fieldFilters = this.getTableFieldFilter(fieldList);
the error appears: "Missing clone or copy constructor for right hand side of equation" With parameters: (VarRecord).
The workaround will be:
var fieldFilters
:=
this.getTableFieldFilter(fieldList);

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Include() syntax in ModScript plus Error: "Single-quoted strings must be 1 character long"
content:
Article ID: S142386

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
ModScript allows both of the following statements to be used to include other files:
$include(myScript) (as it was in AppScript)
include("myScript")
This is currently documented in the AppScript to ModScript migration guide.
When using the old syntax $include(myScript) a validate in Composer throws the following error:
Error: "Single-quoted strings must be 1 character long" in 'myScript'...
Regardless of the validation error the include actually works after a deployment.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This will be added to the ModScript documentation

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim12: SBM2DM Connector:  Error: Could not start transition from state Development to state QA (-1) Transition '' is not valid for transitioning item number 2511 (item 34) in table 1006. [(:TSPrimaryItemStartTransition: Error starting transition Start transition failed.
content:
Article ID: S139453

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After actioning a request to the next state and then attempting to transition this into SBM, the following error can occur in the log files when running the SBM2DM connector:
TEST_CR_7:
Business Mashups Issue: CR02511
Looking for the first transition id with suitable states.
Error: Could not start transition from state <Development> to state <QA> (-1) Transition '' is not valid for transitioning item number 2511 (item 34) in table 1006. [(:TSPrimaryItemStartTransition: Error starting transition Start transition failed.
Updated Dimensions Request FAILED.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error is occurring because there is a Restrict by Rule on the transition within SBM. If the rule is removed, the synchronization works.
The Connector will not work with the Restrict by Rule on a transition. This is currently by design as this is a new feature within SBM.
Workaround: The workaround would be to Restrict by Role on the transition. For example, it should be possible to "Restrict by Role" <Development> to <QA> transition assigning that role to the synchronization user, so the synchronization user is never restricted.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim12: Upgrade: Database schema update fails: ACL4501561E Error: Could not load library libdbio_srv_odbc.so
content:
Article ID: S139635

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The upgrade log files report
Dimensions CM DMDBA 12.2.2 Build 9.289 (Production) at 09:50:34 Friday 15 March 2013
Copyright (c) 1988-2013 Serena Software, Inc. All rights reserved.
ACL4501561E Error: Could not load library libdbio_srv_odbc.so: ld.so.1: dmdba: fatal: libodbc.so.1: open failed: No such file or directory.
ACL4501561E Error: Could not load library libdbio_srv_odbc.so: ld.so.1: dmdba: fatal: libodbc.so.1: open failed: No such file or directory.
Error: unable to connect to the specified DBMS.
A manual dmdba connection reports
dmdba system/password@dimde1
Dimensions CM DMDBA 12.2.2 Build 9.289 (Production) at 10:14:44 Friday 15 March 2013
Copyright (c) 1988-2013 Serena Software, Inc. All rights reserved.
ACL4501561E Error: Could not load library libdbio_srv_odbc.so: ld.so.1: dmdba: fatal: libodbc.so.1: open failed: No such file or directory.
ACL4501561E Error: Could not load library libdbio_srv_odbc.so: ld.so.1: dmdba: fatal: libodbc.so.1: open failed: No such file or directory.
Error: unable to connect to the specified DBMS.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The dm.cfg was missing two lines
DM_DEFAULT_DBMS ORACLE
DBIO_LIBRARY=libdbio_srv_oci8_10201.so
Once these we added the dmdba connection worked and the upgrade could be run correctly.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - RM SyncEngine QC class name error
content:
Article ID: D10095
Defect ID: DEF181960
Product: Dimensions RM 2009 R2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
RM SyncEngine QC class name error

With RM QC10 is the Action section of an event for a QC10 data source sets the QC class name to Requirements instead of Requirement the following error in generated in the log:
DataSource QCSource: Test Director Sync Proxy: Unknown Exception in CDataSource::BeginEvent(LpSyncEvent)

The SyncEngine should generate an error in the syntax check – startup review – and post an error to the syncing.log stating that an invalid QC class name was referenced – this should be a fatal error and the SyncEngine not allowed to start.

Web Service calls to UpdateFileField will fail with the following error:
"Unknown exception encountered"

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The fileObject.Name is blank. In previous versions, if the name was not given, the system would automatically default to the "filename". This default is no longer happening. This is considered a regression.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - IIS hangs with message in the application logs "Unknown Exception occurred during cleanup"
content:
Article ID: D1020
Defect ID: DEF152118
Product: SBM 2008 R3
Fixed In: SBM 2009 R1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
IIS regularly hangs and the application log shows that a required field was needed and an "Unknown Exception occurred during cleanup" similar to below:
The description for Event ID ( 2 ) in Source ( Serena BMS Web Engine ) cannot be found. The local computer may not have the necessary registry information or message DLL files to display messages from a remote computer. You may be able to use the /AUXSOURCE= flag to retrieve this description; see Help and Support for details. The following information is part of the event:
Error occurred in file:
'.\ThreadPool.cpp', line 471.
, Unknown Exception occurred during cleanup.
User: username
Request: TransitionPage&action=warten%%20auf%%20extern&copyprojectid=0&message=Mindestens%%20ein%%20Feld%%20ist%%20ung%c3%bcltig.%0d%0aGeben%%20Sie%%20in%%20Feld%%20%%27Kommentar%%27%%20einen%%20Wert%%20ein.%%20%%28TTwrnIDS_F1_MSG_FIELDS_FIELDREQUIRESVALUE%%29%0d%0a&projectid=6&recordid=4751&recordlockid=33000&reportid=0&state=&tableid=1001&temporaryrecordid=1226572331&transitionid=28&Template=formbody,
.
This is caused by double UTF-8 to UNICODE translation of a warning message. If the message contains a string or fieldname etc... that is high ascii then this is likely to occur.
The string IDS_F1_MSG_FIELDS_FIELDREQUIRESVALUE must also be ensured to have no high-ascii in it.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Fixed in SBM 2008 R2.04
To workaround it ensure that IDS_F1_MSG_FIELDS_FIELDREQUIRESVALUE does not contain any high ascii and that any required field names do not contain high ascii.
Any scripts that have redoshell message to present the transition form again must also be checked to ensure that they contain no high-acii.

[content]
DEF152118

To TOC
Defects Fixed in Version Manager 7.5.1.3
Version Manager Defects Fixed
Long Path to Configuration File Causes VM to Fail [1039271]
A long path (over 200 characters) to a Version Manager configuration file may cause operations to fail. This has been fixed.
Development Interface Defects Fixed
Integration to SCC and Eclipse IDEs Fails on Windows NT [1039154]
The Development Interface does not work correctly with SCC and Eclipse IDEs on Windows NT systems. This has been fixed.
To TOC
Defects Fixed in Version Manager 7.5.1.2
Version Manager GUI, CLI, PCLI, and DTK Defects Fixed
Unable to Add Files to Source Control if the Reference Directory Does Not Exist [1039015]
If a reference directory is defined for a project but that directory does not exist, you will not be able to add files to source control for that project. This has been fixed.
Development Interface Defects Fixed
Visual Studio .NET: Web Projects on Remote Servers Do Not Include Nested Project Structure [1038885]
Visual Studio .NET web projects located on remote servers end up with flat file strucutures when added to source control. This has been fixed.
VM I-Net Defects Fixed

If a reference directory is defined for a project but that directory does not exist, you will not be able to add files to source control for that project. This has been fixed.
To TOC
Defects Fixed in Version Manager 7.5.1.0
Version Manager GUI, CLI, PCLI, and DTK Defects Fixed
PCLI Does Not Create Archives That Have German Characters in the File Name [1031395]
On Windows, if a CodePage other than 1252 was used, the German umlaut characters were not displayed correctly. This has been fixed. Note: you can change or query your CodePage from a Command Prompt using the command
chcp
.
Double Clicking on a Project with No Permissions Locks the GUI [1023776]
If a user was not defined in the access control database for a project and that user double-clicked on a project to open it, the GUI would lock up and display the error message: "Access to this project has been denied because your login account is invalid". This has been fixed.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - PESSoapClient.PESInvoke encountered an unknown exception error.
content:
Article ID: S130775

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
"SYNOPSIS:
The error 'PESSoapClient.PESInvoke encountered an unknown
exception' is returned when attempting to access the Portfolio Edge web site.
This error prevents the logon page from loading.
TROUBLESHOOTING:
Try loading this URL in the web browser on the
server:
http://servername/PESWebServices/PESUserUtilities.asmx
Where
servername is the Portfolio Edge Server.
This URL should return a list of
webservice functions. If it does return the list, try going to this URL on the
web server:
http://localhost/PESWebServices/PESUserUtilities.asmx
This
URL should return a list of webservice functions. If it returns a Page cannot be
displayed error, follow the resolution steps below:
RESOLUTION:
This error is caused by localhost on the web server not being
active. To activate it, go to Start/Settings/Control Panel/Administrative
Tools/Internet Services Manager on the Web server. Right click on the Default
Web Site and choose Properties. On the Web Site tab, the IP Adress should be set
to All Unassigned.
If you would like to have an IP Address assigned, you can
add the following registry entry to the PE web server. The value is a string
value:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Pacific Edge
Software\WebServiceClients]
""Server""=""yourservernamehere""
For example,
to not use localhost, but to use machine network name ""computer01"" the entry
would look like:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Pacific Edge
Software\WebServiceClients]
""Server""=""computer01""
This registry entry
is required on all the machines where the admin tool is
installed."

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Error "Unknown Exception at line 299 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp" is shown when checking in certain files
content:
Article ID: S138140

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
knowledgebase.serena.com/InfoCenter/index
When performing a Checkin / Put or AddFiles operation, certain files, depending on their size or content, may trigger one of the followings errors:
Unknown Exception at line 294 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp
(when the client is running VM 8.0.x - 8.1.4.2)
Unknown Exception at line 299 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp
(when the client is running VM 8.2.0.0 or newer, validated through 8.6.0.0)
Unknown Exception at line 1133 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp
(when the client is running VM 8.0.x - 8.1.4.2)
Unknown Exception at line 1138 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp
(when the client is running VM 8.2.0.0 - VM 8.4.6.0)
Unknown Exception at line 1196 in VMNMArchiveFile.cpp
(when the client is running VM 8.5.0.0 or newer, validated through 8.6.0.0)
file
, Error communicating with file server "http[s]://
server
:
port
/serenafs/FileServer;": Unexpected end of file, no input, or timeout receiving data..
An error occured while reading archive <The archive was not found <
ArchivePath
>: <
ArchivePath
(The system cannot find the path specified)>.>: <
ArchivePath
>.
(when seen immediately following a check-in)
Once this error has been seen, the client
may
:
Become unresponsive
Complain about the inability to read Configuration Files (.cfg)
Show: Warning: file "
ConfigFilePath
" was accessed from "/" in File Server-based Project Database "
ProjectDatabasePath
", but this path does not use a File Server.
Show files with a red-x icon, indicating the archive files have become inaccessible.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This error is almost always caused by the network preventing the file from being uploaded to the File Server, or an unsplit archive exceeding its maximum size.
The following are the most common causes seen by the PVCS VM Support Team:
Interference by Network / End-Point-Security utilities.
Check real-time Network security applications (and to a lesser extent Antivirus solutions). The data-stream that contains the file contents may (incorrectly) be flagged an attack, causing the File Server connection to get severed. This generally shows up in the logs of these applications. Look for events that coincide with the time of failure.
As these utilities are often (though not always) limited to scanning data on ports 80 and 8080, switching the File Server clients to use the alternate File Server port, 8090, may work as a way to avoid this problem, at least until a permanent solution is in place. The File Server normally also listens on port 8090, so testing this is as simple as changing the port used by the client from the desktop client using Admin -> File Server. Should this make the problem go away, a permanent solution could involve adding the File Server to the exception list.
As End-Point-Security utilities typically cannot view contents of encrypted data streams (SSL), switching the File Server connection from HTTP on port 8080 or 8090 to HTTPS on port 8443 is also an option. When doing this, please review KB doc
S142991
regarding certificate validation requirements.
Network Interface Card (NIC) problems, specifically related to the use of Auto-negotiate when the PC is equipped with a Gigabit-capable NIC.
If the NIC of the PC that is used to check the code in is one that supports Gigabit Ethernet, go to the adapter settings and check if the adapter speed is set to "Auto" or "Auto negotiate". If it is, close all network related applications and change the setting to "100 Mb/s Full-Duplex" or, if that setting causes a loss of network connectivity, "100 Mb/s Half-Duplex". Now try the checkin again.
If this does not work, try the same for the NIC of the system running the Version Manager File Server, a.k.a. the Serena VM Web Application Server, and also investigate the intermediate devices on the network between the client PC and the server (switches, hubs and routers).
This has been the solution for a number of customers whose network was not equipped to fully support Gigabit Etherne

title: Serena Knowledgebase - DimCM:   NCL4501392E Uncaught unknown exception and NCL0004747E Error: Failed to execute RPC 23
content:
Article ID: S142598

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request with ALF events enabled for the basedatabase and/or project/stream, the following error will occur:
Action Request - The request QLARIUS_CR_168 has been forwarded to DINESH, DMSYS
NCL4501392E Uncaught unknown exception
NCL0004747E Error: Failed to execute RPC 23
The request will be actioned but the error will occur.
Note: If ALF events are not enabled/configured for the basedatabase and/or project/stream, the error does not occur. This problem will also occur when creating a request.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error occurs because a date/time field in Admin Console for this request type has an invalid Max Length. When creating a date/time attribute, the Max Length column in the Global Properties area of the attribute is not filled in automatically with a Max Length of 11. The Date attribute format value is an 11-character date in the format of DD-MMM-YYYY, i.e., 15-JAN-2018.
If the Max Length is set to greater than 11, the above error will occur.
To fix the issue, change the Max Length on the Attribute for the Request Type to 11.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: Running a modscript fails with error: ERR #34 "Unavailable" and "Unknown Exception"
content:
Article ID: D28112
Defect ID: DEF349290
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2 Patch 1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When trying to run a modscript via web service, Shell.Context() gives an error such as the following in the Event Viewer:
Error occurred in file:
'D:\bld\BM_MAINLINE\BM_MAINLINE-AEX64\Application_Engine\software\teamtrack\source\Process\SLError.cpp', line 310.
User: Id 8, 'Administrator'
RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "scriptIncludeParentSubmit" (id=9)
(called from runmodscript-SOAP context)
ModScript Error -- ERR #34
Line -1> { Unavailable }
Error: "Unknown Exception"
This happens if the modscript is executed by SBM Web Services via:
Run from an orchestration in modscript context.
Run as a scheduled task.
A transition with a modscript action is triggered via SBM Web Services.
Example of a problem modscript:
// Test value of
Shell.Context() cannot be used directly in comparison operator.
if (Shell.Context() == "runmodscript-SOAP") {
var test=Shell.ScriptName();
Ext.LogErrorMsg( "Testing Logging ");
}

View all Defects
ID:
D28112
Published:
16 April 2024
Updated:
06 August 2025
Defect Id
DEF349290
Originally Reported Against
SBM 12.1
Resolved In
SBM 12.2 Patch 1
Description
When trying to run a modscript via web service, Shell.Context() gives an error such as the following in the Event Viewer:
Error occurred in file:
'D:\bld\BM_MAINLINE\BM_MAINLINE-AEX64\Application_Engine\software\teamtrack\source\Process\SLError.cpp', line 310.
User: Id 8, 'Administrator'
RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "scriptIncludeParentSubmit" (id=9)
(called from runmodscript-SOAP context)
ModScript Error -- ERR #34
Line -1> { Unavailable }
Error: "Unknown Exception"
This happens if the modscript is executed by SBM Web Services via:
Run from an orchestration in modscript context.
Run as a scheduled task.
A transition with a modscript action is triggered via SBM Web Services.
Example of a problem modscript:
// Test value of
Shell.Context() cannot be used directly in comparison operator.
if (Shell.Context() == "runmodscript-SOAP") {
var test=Shell.ScriptName();
Ext.LogErrorMsg( "Testing Logging ");
}
Resolution

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Unknown object property  --  ERR #8 when using Keys or Items method of the Scripting.Dictionary object
content:
Article ID: S138684 [DESCRIPTION] Description When using the Dictionary.Scripting object, the AE returns the following error: RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "SampleTwo" (id=28) (called from pre-transition context) Unknown object property -- ERR #8 Line 25> Next The sample code used follows: dim d,a,i,s set d=Server.CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d.Keys s = s & a & "
" next set d=nothing [RESOLUTION] Resolution AppScript does not support the use of the Items and Keys methods. Instead, use For Each on the dictionary object to return the key for each item. The value can be referenced using the standard dictionary object Item property. See sample code. dim d,a,s set d=CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d s = s & d.Item(a) & chr(13) & chr(10) next Call Shell.Item.SetFieldValue("DESCRIPTION",s) set d=nothing [content] When using the Dictionary.Scripting object, the AE returns the following error: RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "SampleTwo" (id=28) (called from pre-transition context) Unknown object property -- ERR #8 Line 25> Next The sample code used follows: dim d,a,i,s set d=Server.CreateObject("Scripting.Dictionary") d.Add "nor", "Norway" d.Add "ita", "Italy" for each a in d.Keys s = s & a & "

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Example Script for AppRecord object in Mashup/Team Script Reference is faulty
content:
Article ID: S134884

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
The example script on the Page 60 of Mashup Script Reference ,in the AppRecord section which begins in page 59, is faulty.
Its execution will produce the following error message :
"
RUNTIME ERROR IN SCRIPT "Aux" (id=24)
(called from post-transition context)
Wrong number of arguments -- ERR #7
Line 4> Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
"
This is because the tableId variable in line 3 and 4 of the script has not been declared .
This is the script as it appears in the Mashup Script Reference.
"
Dim myRecord, myLong
REM Create a Contact record
tableId = Ext.TableId("TS_CONTACTS")
Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
myRecord.SetName("First Middle LastName")
myLong = myRecord.Add()
If myLong = 0 Then
Call Ext.LogErrorMsg("Error adding Contact.")
End If
"
The script should look as follows :
"
Dim myRecord, myLong ,
tableId
REM Create a Contact record
tableId = Ext.TableId("TS_CONTACTS")
Set myRecord = Ext.CreateAppRecord (tableId)
myRecord.SetName("First Middle LastName")
myLong = myRecord.Add()
If myLong = 0 Then
Call Ext.LogErrorMsg("Error adding Contact.")
End If
"
This error has been carried over from the Teamscript Reference and it needs correcting there as well.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Troubleshooting: Notification using Run Script does not kick off the AppScript (SBM 10.1+)
content:
Article ID: S139161

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
This document is designed to help you to troubleshoot notifications that run an AppScript. If the AppScript appears to fail or does not run at all, use these steps to narrow down the problem.
NOTE:
The notification engine for SBM was changed in the 10.1 release. This document will focus on the SBM 10.1 release.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Step 1: Check the ns.log file
Check the ns.log file (by default in, C:\Program Files\Serena\SBM\Common\jboss405\server\default\work\jboss.web\localhost\notificationsrv\log) for errors. Below are 2 examples of common errors you will find in the log.
Example A:
In this example, the error is "An error occurred while sending an event", but it does not tell you what the error is. The trick in this situation are the lines BEFORE the notification. Here you will see "Invalid User ID". This is the user ID that SBM will use if the script needs to access data within the system. This user ID is set inside Configurator, under Mail Services, on the Notification Server tab, in the field called Web Services Authentication User. This user must also have the System permission called Connect Using the API.
ERROR 2012-09-25 13:28:18,920 [notificationsrv.ScriptEventService.Events] -- com.serena.core.runtime.LocalizedCoreException: Failed to obtain security token: Invalid User ID or Password
INFO 2012-09-25 13:28:18,920 [notificationsrv.ScriptEventService.Events] -- Script for notificationID: 137, eventID: 21 , userID: 0
ERROR 2012-09-25 13:28:18,922 [notificationsrv.EventService.Events] -- An error occurred while sending an event for the 'MyNotificationTest' notification.
INFO 2012-09-25 13:28:18,922 [notificationsrv.EventService.Events] -- Removing event 21, Tbl:ItemId 1000:7, from Events Table because there's no escalation conditions
Example B:
This is another example where the log says an error occurred, but does not give any information about the error. This is usually because there is an error in the script itself. Continue with Step 2 below for more troubleshooting ideas.
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:00 [Notifications] -- Notification 'My Notification' being sent on Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829 for all recipients
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:00 [Notifications] -- Added event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, to events table for notification 'My Notification'
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Initial send for event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, sending notification 'My Notification'
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Script for notificationID: 178, eventID: 5784 , userID: 28
ERROR 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- An error occurred while sending an event for the 'My Notification' notification.
INFO 21-09-2012 08:12:15 [Events] -- Removing event 5784, Tbl:ItemId 1018:3829, from Events Table because there's no escalation conditions
NOTE:
You can increase the amount of information that is logged by changing the Logging Level in Configurator under Mail Services on the Notification Server tab.
Step 2: Check the application engine log
In order for the notification to run a script, it must be passed to the Application Engine for processing. The Application Engine writes most of errors in the Event Viewer. Check the Event Viewer application log for any messages that happened at the time the notification ran. See below for example error messages.
Step 3: Run the script from a transition
If you still have not been able to find the problem, temporarily add a transition to your workflow that has an Action that will run the script, deploy to staging environment, and move an item through this transition. Any errors will be displayed at the top of the browser. See below for example error messages. REMEMBER: Some objects may not be valid in the context of a transition, so this test may not work in your situation.
Step 4: Simplify the script
If you still have not been able to find the problem, try simplifying the script. Maybe take out EVERY line in the script, and only include the following line:
Call Ext.L

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9: When attempting to perform a release baseline from PC Client the resulting  error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp' can be caused by a white space character preceding the id.
content:
Article ID: S119812

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the
id.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the id.
Run the
following script to determine character length in the id name.
sqlplus basedb/password@<sid>
SQL> select
baseline_id, length(baseline_id), base_seq_no
from baseline_history
where
baseline_id like 'build5';
SQL> select obj_id, length (obj_id),
obj_spec_uid
from bln_spec_catalogue
where obj_id like
'build5';
You will receive output similiar to below:
SQL>
select baseline_id, length(baseline_id), base_seq_no from baseline_history where
baseline_id like 'build5';
BASELINE_ID LENGTH(BASELINE_ID)
BASE_SEQ_NO
------------------------- -------------------
-----------
build 5 8 4960359
SQL> select obj_id,
length(obj_id), obj_spec_uid from bln_spec_catalogue where obj_id like
'CNARESULT';
OBJ_ID LENGTH(OBJ_ID) OBJ_SPEC_UID
--------------
------------
build 5 7 4960360
As you can see the entry in baseline
history is one character longer than the entry in bln_spec_catalogue. The
customer should run DBL 'ECS: BUILD 5' (note preceding white space chacter is
included) and baseline should be deleted.

[content]
When attempting to perform a release baseline from Desktop Client
the resulting error: 'Create Release - Error: Delete failed for pcms263_1.tmp'
can be caused by a white space character preceding the
id.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - VM:  Error: Installshield scripting runtime 1607
content:
Article ID: S123171

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
VM: Error: Installshield scripting runtime 1607

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Installshield scripting runtime 1607
When installing VM 6.8.10, users may receive this type of Installshield error message when they don't have privileges to write to the system registry and/or Windows directories.
This error can appear during initial stage of installation, when Version Manager attempts create temporary files or Windows folders.
Login as a user with Administrator privileges and run the install again.
