title: Serena Knowledgebase - SBM: HTTP status server 500 - Internal Server Error when trying to get OAuth2 access tokens for mailbox/SMTP server
content:
Article ID: D28280
Defect ID: DEF349753
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
In the SBM Application Administrator > Emails > Mailboxes or Mail Servers, it is necessary to acquire access tokens for O365. On token refresh, you may receive a HTTP error after logging in.
The TOMCAT.LOG will display an error like this:
2025-05-15 15:09:39,022 ERROR [ajp-nio-0.0.0.0-6009-exec-6] [org.apache.catalina.core.ContainerBase.[Catalina].[localhost].[/commonsvc].[resourcemgmt]:175] [::] -- Servlet.service() for servlet [resourcemgmt] in context with path [/commonsvc] threw exception [Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]] with root cause
org.apache.jasper.JasperException: Unable to get JAR resource [/WEB-INF/views/jakarta.tags.core] containing TLD: [java.io.FileNotFoundException]
at org.apache.jasper.compiler.DefaultErrorHandler.jspError(DefaultErrorHandler.java:37) ~[jasper.jar:10.1.34]
This is caused by an older jakarta library.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
WORK AROUND
1. Stop Tomcat server
2. Go to ~
\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib and copy 2 files:
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-3.0.1.jar
jakarta.servlet.jsp.jstl-api-3.0.0.jar
3. Copy these 2 jar files to locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\WEB-INF\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\WEB-INF\lib
4. Optionally, you can remove next files:
taglibs-standard-impl-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-jstlel-1.2.5.jar
taglibs-standard-spec-1.2.5.jar
from the next locations:
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\idp\lib
~\SBM\Common\tomcat\server\default\webapps\commonsvc\lib
5. Start Tomcat server

DEF349753

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Error: Root element is missing - importing a Process App into Composer after generating it from a Get from AE if any of your Process Apps contain a Reset Data Source
content:
Article ID: D24251
Defect ID: DEF299981
Product: SBM 11.2
Fixed In: SBM 11.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you create any Rest Data Source in ANY Process app and deploy and then do a Get from AE ALL process apps will now fail when importing to Composer. They all contain this Rest Data Source (including the global) and will fail with error "Root element is missing".
To reproduce:
(1) Take any Process App and add the Rest Data Source in Composer.
(2) Deploy
(3) Do an get of ANY Process App in the environment and try to import into Composer. This change to one process APP affects the GET of ALL process Apps.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
These generated Process Apps from a Get can be fixed. Please contact Support for help.

[content]
DEF299981

title: Serena Knowledgebase - After upgrading to VM 8.4.6.0 Combo Hotfix 1 or VM 8.5.0.0, Version Manager can no longer access archives in paths containing a subdirectory named ROOT
content:
Article ID: D19825
Defect ID: DEF256144
Product: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.00
Fixed In: PVCS Version Manager 8.5.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If the path to an archive contains a subdirectory named ROOT, attempts to access this archive from VM 8.4.6.0 Combo Hotfix 1 or VM 8.5.0.0 will fail. The error messages show that the subdirectory ROOT is treated as the beginning of the archive path.
For example, if the Project database contains the archive path \\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1
\ROOT\
File1.txt-arc then an attempt to use this file will fail with the error:
[07-22-2014 03:03:18.184] VmEntityValidateListener: addAnswerToCache=false, exception=Serena PVCS Version Manager could not read the information stored in the archive "\\File1.txt-arc". Please contact your Serena administrator for further assistance.
Error: /Project1/ROOT/File1.txt:
/Project1/ROOT/File1.txt
Serena PVCS Version Manager could not read the information stored in the archive "\\File1.txt-arc". Please contact your Serena administrator for further assistance.
You can get similar error messages trying to access a Configuration File or Access Control Database that is located in a path containing a subdirectory named "ROOT".

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
As a workaround, change the paths in the VM Project Database that contain the subdirectory "ROOT" such that this directory is referenced using a different case variant, like "
Root
" or "
root
". If the file system and/or the File Server are configured for case-insensitive file access, making this change will permit the affected clients to use the files in their original location, without the need to rename the "ROOT" subdirectories.
To update archive locations for individual files, you can execute the command:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"
PathToProjectDatabase
" -id"
UserID
:
Password
" -a"
ArchivePath
" "
EntityPathToFile
"
For the error message in the example above, the following command would solve the problem:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB" -id"Admin" -a"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1\
Root
\File1.txt-arc" "/Project1/
ROOT
/File1.txt"
Note that only the case of the archive directory was changed, not the case of the name of the subproject containing the file (the entity path).
To change the default location on the project itself, so newly added files won't run into problems either, use the same command on the project instead of the file:
pcli SetArchiveLocation -pr"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB" -id"Admin" -a"\\vmfs_w2k8\SampleDB\archives\Project1\
Root
" "/Project1/
ROOT
"

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim CM 2009 R2: UNIX - Web Client allows files to be renamed to contain double quotes
content:
Article ID: D12191
Defect ID: DEF199286
Product: Dimensions CM 2009 R2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.1 Dimensions CM 12.2 Release Manager 2.0 Release Manager 2.1 Release Vault 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Using the Web Client you can rename an item filename so that it includes a double quote. This then causes problems performing a Get of the item, resulting in the following error: -
java.io.FileNotFoundException: C:\test".c (The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect)
The problem seems to occur using UNIX Dimensions servers only. This cannot be reproduced on Windows as you get the following message when you attempt to rename the file to include the double quote: -
Set Filename - COR0005885E Error: Filename tes"t1.dat contains one or more of the following invalid characters: "*|?<>:

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Orchestration fails when WSDL contains multiple parts (xsds)
content:
Article ID: D12208
Defect ID: DEF199355
Product: SBM 2009 R4
Found In: Service Manager 1.0
Fixed In: SBM 2009 R4.01 Service Manager 1.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
If you have an orchestration that calls a webservice for which the WSDL contains multiple parts (i.e. xsd's) then when this web service call is reached in the orchestration the orchestration will fail with an errors similar to the following:
(1) The orchestration actually fails with "Orchestration workflow failed. Contact SBM administrator ....".
(2) The server log contains an error starting with similar to the following:
"00000:OPENARMWorkflow ERROR 2011-03-02 13:54:20,308 org.jbpm.graph.def.GraphElement -- action threw exception: null
java.lang.NullPointerException"
(3) The Common Log will show the following DEBUG error before the orchestration fails as per (1)
WSDLException: faultCode=OTHER_ERROR: Unable to resolve imported document at 'resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl'.: This file was not found: file:/C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Common/jboss405/bin/resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl: java.io.FileNotFoundException: This file was not found: file:/C:/Program Files/Serena/SBM/Common/jboss405/bin/resources/xml/ea449449-8533-41b1-8e66-cc681a28443b/dmwebservices.wsdl

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9:21043-WS_ROOT_HAS_ITEM Error: The Work Set root contains an Item named %s
content:
Article ID: S124525

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
KM-Dim9:21043-WS_ROOT_HAS_ITEM Error: The Work Set root contains an Item named %s

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Please gather any applicable information from the list below and contact Dimensions Support for help with this error message.
What you need to troubleshoot:
A screen shot of the full error message. Note: The error message text in your error may vary slightly from this article.
The version and patch level of:
The Dimensions server
The Dimensions server Operating System
The Dimensions client
The Dimensions client Operating System
The Browser (if the error occurred using the Admin Console or Web Client)
The Java in use (if the error occurred using the Admin Console or Web Client)
The IDE used, if applicable
The Database platform and version being used
The exact list of steps to recreate the error message
A detailed control plan report
Any additional details or background information related to this problem
Additional information that may be helpful:
A detailed control plan report
Event logs (Windows OS only)
/var/adm/messages file (Unix Only)
Oracle alert log
Please answer these questions about the problem:
Is the problem reproducible every time? If not, can you determine any factors that cause the error message (ie: the CPU is maxed out when the error occurs)?
Does the problem occur on all machines? If not, is there anything consistent about the machines it does occur on (ie: only windows XP machines)?
Does the problem occur on for all users? If not, is there anything consistent about the users who get the error (ie: is it only users with the developer role)?
How to contact Dimensions Support:
Please use the link below for information about contacting Dimensions Support:
http://support.serena.com/kb/articles/dimensions/kb70712.asp

title: Serena Knowledgebase - VM: PCLI - The project root could not be loaded: "ProjectDBPath" -or- The project root could not be loaded: ""
content:
Article ID: S120118

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When running a PCLI command the following message is returned:
The project root could not be loaded: "
ProjectDBPath
".

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
If the error contains a non-empty
ProjectDBPath
then this path is invalid or the Project Database Root is not recognized.
First check the
ProjectDBPath
by opening the Project Database in the GUI and taking note of the path that is specified. This is the path that needs to be passed to the PCLI command, either by using the option
-pr"
ProjectDBPath
"
or by using the environment variable
PCLI_PR
and assigning the value "
ProjectDBPath
" to it.
If the Project Database is using Windows-style paths and PCLI is running on Linux/UNIX, replace all backslash characters (\) with forward slash ones (/) to avoid the shell for treating them as escape characters and mangling the path before it gets to PCLI.
If the Version Manager File Server is used to access your project databases, and other users of that File Server can access the identical
ProjectDBPath
without getting this error, then PCLI may lack the information it needs to locate the File Server. The process that Version Manager uses to locate the File Server is documented in KB doc
S134330
.
Possible culprits that prevent Version Manager from finding the File Server:
Missing File Server definition in
servers.ini
file or not having this file at all.
Missing reference to servers.ini in the file
ISLV.INI
(Windows) or
.islvrc
(Linux/UNIX) or not having this file at all.
Note that on Linux/UNIX every user needs to have his or her own copy of .islvrc in their home directory.
See KB doc
S138903
to learn where Version Manager is looking for the ISLV.INI / .islvrc file.
Users of VM 8.4.4 or older running in setuid mode on Linux/UNIX may also run into a permissions problem. See KB doc
D12816
for more information.
If
ProjectDBPath
in the error message shows up empty, as in:
The project root could not be loaded: "".
then one of the following as happened:
The option
-pr
was not specified and no
PCLI_PR
variable was defined.
The option
-pr
was specified as
─pr
or
—pr
meaning the option does not start with a regular DASH (
"-"
, a.k.a. the minus character) but one of the following:
An EN-DASH
"─"
(Unicode U+2013 / ASCII code 150 or 0x96 in CodePage 1250 / ASCII code 196 or 0xC4 in CodePage 1252)
An EM-DASH "—" (Unicode U+2014 / ASCII code 151 or 0x97 in CodePage 1250)
Although these characters look like a dash they are fundamentally different and not recognized by command line utilities as option lead-in characters.
This can happen when commands are copied from Microsoft Office or Outlook documents, when those tools have been configured to replace certain characters with nicer looking equivalents (dashes, smart quotes, etc.). While the replacement characters may be more pleasing to the eye, they are not functionally equivalent and will cause failures when used from command line tools like PCLI.

[content]
When running a PCLI command the following message is returned:
The project root could not be loaded: "
ProjectDBPath
".

[contentbody]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - SRA: Agent installation fails on linux when AgentOwner is not root
content:
Article ID: S141159

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Silent install of agent on Linux fails with no error code when the options file contains a non-root user such as the following:
-V AgentOwner="serena"
The installLocation and the IS_DESTINATION were both set to /opt/serena/Release_Automation_Agent, however the "serena" folder did not yet exist.
The log files in the /tmp folder contained the runWrapped log and this indicated an error attempting to invoke the java.
After the installer failed, the Release_Automation_Agent folder and subfolders existed, but the core folder was empty.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The installation script was being run by user root. Therefore, at the start of the installation the folder serena is created underneath of opt. The serena folder is owned by root and the only user with permissions on serena was root.
The Release_Automation_Agent folder was also created. The installer set this to be owned by serena and the permissions were set appropriately on this folder. Because the _jvm folder (underneath Release_Automation_Agent) was still under the path of "serena", the user serena could not access this folder to start the jvm.
To resolve this problem, prior to running the installation, create the serena folder and perform a chmod 755 on this folder. Then invoke the silent installer.

[content]
Silent install of agent on Linux fails with no error code when the options file contains a non-root user such as the following:

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Can Tracker point to the PVCSTRK.INI file in a different location than the system root?
content:
Article ID: S119328

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Setting Tracker and Notify to point to the initialization file (pvcstrk.ini) in a different location than the system root
On certain scenarios, Tracker users do not have permissions on the system root directory (C:\Winnt or C:\Windows) of the machines due to their company's security policy.

Tracker points to the initialization file PVCSTRK.INI file that is created in the system root of the machine while Tracker is installed. This file contains personal settings for Tracker, Tracker Admin, and Notify. Tracker and Notify applications read these settings when they are started.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The initialization file 'pvcstrk.ini' can be copied
from the system root directory to a different location where the Tracker users
have full control permission and, the Tracker, Notify applications can be made
to point to the file in the new location.
Following are the steps to copy the 'pvcstrk.ini' file
from the system root directory to a different location and set Tracker GUI,
Tracker Admin and Notify to point to the file in the new location:
1.
Copy the 'pvcstrk.ini' file from C:\Winnt to another drive (Example: D:\PVCS\newpvcstrk.ini.)
2.
Navigate to Start | Programs | PVCS Tracker | PVCS Tracker.
3.
Right click and select 'Properties'.
4.
The 'Target' option box will have the location of the Tracker executable file
(Example: 'D:\PVCS\Tracker 7.1\Tracker\nt\pvcstkn.exe')
5.
Add the path of the 'newpvcstrk.ini' file to the end of the Tracker executable
path with a '/I' variable (Example: The 'Target' option should have a value like
'D:\PVCS\Tracker 7.1\Tracker\nt\pvcstkn.exe'/I D:\PVCS\newpvcstrk.ini.).
This
will enable the Tracker GUI read the 'newpvcstrk.ini' file.
Repeat
the same steps selecting Tracker Admin in step 2 (Navigate to Start | Programs |
PVCS Tracker | PVCS Tracker Admin). This will enable Tracker Admin read the
'newpvcstrk.ini' file when it is opened.
Also,
follow the same steps selecting PVCS Notify in step 2 (Navigate to Start |
Programs | PVCS Tracker | PVCS Notify). This will enable Notify read the
'newpvcstrk.ini' file when it is opened.

[content]

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

existing object

synonyms

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

[content]
DEF349678

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

synonyms

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

existing object

synonyms

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:
Article ID: S136172

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force
Applies To
Dimensions CM

10.1.3.1

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

DEF257172

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

[content]
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured

existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,

Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
Resolution
The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - "ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option" Error in Tomcat.log after upgrade to SBM 12.2.1
content:
Article ID: D28244
Defect ID: DEF349678
Product: SBM 12.1
Fixed In: SBM 12.2.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
After an upgrade to 12.2.1 or 12.2.2, the Tomcat.log shows the following error(s) in the tomcat.log each time Tomcat is restarted.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,095 ERROR [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:311] [::] -- Failed doUpgrade ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:268] [::] -- Serena Event Log failed to initialize.
2025-04-09 18:47:51,096 FATAL [Catalina-utility-10] [com.serena.eventlog.EventManager:269] [::] -- Exception: ORA-02158: invalid CREATE INDEX option

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Workaround:
Stop SBM Tomcat service
Execute the following queries on the Orchestration database:
CREATE INDEX IDX_EL_EVENT_SVC_FLW_PRC_DATA_EV_ID ON EL_EVENT_SRVC_FLW_PROCSNG_DATA(EVENT_ID);
UPDATE EL_EVENT_LOG_SETTING SET SETTING_VALUE='11' WHERE SETTING_NAME='SCHEMA_VERSION';
Start Tomcat Service.

[content]
DEF349678

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10: TT2Dim:  Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected
content:
Article ID: D1879
Defect ID: DEF142777
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Dimensions Connector for TeamTrack fails with ORA-01795 error if more than 1000 design parts are selected.
This problem is reported against Dimensions 10.1.1 and 10.1.2 in configurations that use Oracle for the Dimensions database.
The Customer has encountered the following in the log file. Note the log file will be in the directory: %DM_ROOT%\Integrations\TeamTrack\. The name of the log file will usually be something like 1.log where 1 is replaced by the link number in question.
snippet from the log file:
New TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Updated TeamTrack issues since the last run: 0
Could not get list of new and updated Dimensions items SELECT distinct ch_doc_id INTO :O1.S.128 FROM cm_catalogue cmc, cm_attributes cma, cm_impacts im, part_catalogue pc, part_spec_catalogue psc WHERE cmc.ch_uid = cma.ch_uid AND (cma.attr_42 IS NULL OR LENGTH(cma.attr_42) = 0) AND (cma.attr_45 IS NULL OR cma.attr_45 NOT LIKE '%Deleted%') AND
[note: remainder of query is removed from this solution for reasons of brevity, but the query will contain over 1000 design part names in the query.]
Error 023: Querying CD's to create failed. (QueryCDsToCreate) DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred
SQL-1795(01047830) ORA-01795: maximum number of expressions in a list is 1000
Could not get all TT items.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This problem is caused by having more than 1000 design parts included in the "link" used by the connector. Because the connector is configured to list a design part and then include all children parts or no children parts, this error is likely to occur when selecting a design part with many descendants.
This problem is resolved in Dimensions CM 10.1.3.
To workaround this problem, prior to Dimensions CM 10.1.3, reduce/remove sufficient design parts to drop the number in use to something less than 1000.

[content]
DEF142777

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Initial project create fails; ORA-01017: invalid username/password
content:
Article ID: D19953
Defect ID: DEF257172
Product: Dimensions RM 12.2
Found In: Development Manager 1.2
Fixed In: Development Manager 1.2 Dimensions RM 12.2

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Scenario:
Initial project import fails with an error “The log filecould not be created.
Do you want tocontinue without generating a log file?”
Other errors are generated, among them “ORA-01017: invalid username/password;logon denied.”
The problem indicates that the security.dat file was notupdated when the icprojects and icadmin passwords were created, the most likelyreason is that the user was not running as an administrator or lacked create/writepermission to the security.dat file.
Do not attempt to fix this by running RM Manage again.
Check the file access permissions on thesecurity.dat file or contact Serena Support for assistance.
Please note that when running RM Manage immediately after aninstall, be sure to right-click on the executable and “Run as Administrator”.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: 1019-DBIO:  oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table name'
content:
Article ID: S119923

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
1019-DBIO: oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1019-DBIO:oracle error occured SQL-0904 3531 ORA-08102: index key not
found, obj# 5669, dba 67170180 '2' 9505- failed to delete from table 'table
name'
Rebuild the index for that table only. You will have to run
CREATE_PCMS_INDEX.sql in its entirety first then execute the lines which apply
to that table name only or rebuild all indices for the entire database. Further
detailed instruction can be found in the Database Adminstrators
Guide.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim: ORA-01502: index "index_name" or partition of such index is in unusable state  COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets
content:
Article ID: S140458

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When building an item, an error occurs of
ORA-01502: index <index_name> or partition of such index is in unusable state
COR0004731E Error: failed to query details for build targets

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is caused by a problem with the Oracle index.
To fix it, you can run this :
sqlplus
basedbname/password@<sid
>
SQL> alter index index_name rebuild;
SQL> commit;
Note: Replace the index_name with the index referenced in the error message

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error: 1019-DBIO ORACLE Error occured SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an existing object 11907-Failed to create User synonyms
content:
Article ID: S119717

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User
synonyms

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Process Modeller: Creating new user and get error:
1019-DBIO: ORACLE
Error occured
SQL-1E36-26(11747) ORA-00955 name is already used by an
existing object
11907-Failed to create User synonyms
While we
don't know how this happened, we suspect that the database has cases where a
given user or user role has been granted access to more than one base database.
In order to confirm this, run the following query:
sqlplus
system/<password>
select grantee, table_name, count(distinct
owner)
from sys.dba_tab_privs
group by grantee, table_name
having
count(distinct owner) > 1;
The above query may take a moment to run,
however, if it turns up any rows then we are on to something.
If the
above query turns up rows then we'd like to do the following:
select
grantee, table_name, owner
from sys.dba_tab_privs
where
grantee='<grantee listed in above query>'
and
table_name='<table_name listed in above query>';
This will
show us what has been granted to the specific user or role from all
schemas.
Lastly, we look through the results of the second query (above)
to find where the same table name is granted by more than one schema.
In
this case, the second table was:
SYSTEM.RELEASE_ITEMS
We
speculated that this table resulted from a mistake in an import
operation.
We corrected this by dropping the table
system.release_items.
If the offending extra reference had been to
another base database, we could have merely revoked the grants with a statement
similar to
SQL> revoke ALTER, DELETE, EXECUTE, INDEX, INSERT, READ,
REFERENCES, SELECT, UPDATE on <owner>.<table_name> from
<grantee>;

1019-DBIO ORACLE
Error occured

existing object

synonyms

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10:  DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request
content:

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When actioning a request, the following error may occur:
DBI0004527E DBIO: Database I/O error occurred SQL-904(85024) ORA-00904: "RX"."RTM_COLLECTION": invalid identifier COR1900099E Error: Query failed for evolving/fixed RM relationsips for request

The above error may occur if the database has not been upgraded to the proper version of Dimensions.
To fix,
1. Log into DMDBA as the system user, similar to the following:
DMDBA system/<password>@<SID>
2. Run the following:
Upgrade <basedatabasename> /force

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10:  During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create command fails '...ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command'
content:
Article ID: S128849

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
Error received:
ERROR at line 1:
ORA-00901: invalid CREATE command

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
During install process and creating an Oracle user for the import, the create
command fails as noted on Page 570 of the Serena Dimensions CM 10.1 Installation
Guide, dmcm_installation.pdf.
The command shown in the Installation guide is incorrect. It should
read:
SQL> create
user
<oracle_user> identified by
2> <oracle_user_password> default tablespace
3> pcms_data
4> temporary tablespace pcms_temp
5> quota unlimited on pcms_data;
SQL> grant connect, resource to <oracle_user>;
This has been reported as a Documentation defect
(DEF07676).

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim10: How to configure 2 Dimensions server to run on different port numbers
content:
Article ID: D4449
Defect ID: DEF106020
Product: Dimensions CM 10.1.1

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Setting the pcms_sdp port to a non-default value results in the following:
Item library cannot be defined, even with the node name set to <host>:<port> and having set the correct network object.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
This is a configuration issue.
DM_SERVICE_PCMS_SDPx is a Windows-only symbol.
If you want to run two Dimensions servers on the same machine on ports 671 and 673, then you need to perform the following.
1. Stop both instances.
2. Delete the pcms_sdp entry from /etc/services
3. Delete DM_SERVICE_PCMS_SDPx entry from $DM_ROOT/dm.cfg
For each instance do the following replacing <port> with the appropriate port number:
4. echo "pcms_sdp <port>/tcp" > $DM_ROOT/services.dm
5. add "DM_SERVICES_FILE %DM_ROOT%/services.dm" to each dm.cfg file
6. ensure that "-service" parameter has the default "pcms_sdp" value in DM_ROOT/dfs/listener.dat, i.e.
-service pcms_sdp
7. Restart Dimensions.
If you want an instance configured one port to talk to another instance on another port, then you must login into the first instance and define a network connection from the first instance node to the second instance node using the port number of the second instance as the network object name.
There is no need to edit database manually.

DEF106020

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Common Scheduler shuts down if DB server is cycled - Failed to obtain DB connection from data source 'myDS': java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' ... Could not create connection
content:
Article ID: D13450
Defect ID: DEF175841
Product: SBM 2009 R1.03

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Steps to Reproduce:
1. Run Application Repository
2. Recycle your Database server so that all connections are dropped
3. Notice that error messages will start appearing in the log regarding lost connections from common scheduler.
Actual Result:
The following messages will appear in server.log (or maybe Mashupmgr.log) and all activities will fail to run.
org.quartz.JobPersistenceException: Failed to obtain DB connection from data source 'myDS': java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' org.jboss.util.NestedSQLException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.); - nested throwable: (org.jboss.resource.JBossResourceException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.)) [See nested exception: java.sql.SQLException: Could not retrieve datasource via JNDI url 'java:serenaAdminDsn' org.jboss.util.NestedSQLException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.); - nested throwable: (org.jboss.resource.JBossResourceException: Could not create connection; - nested throwable: (java.sql.SQLException: I/O Error: DB server closed connection.))]
Expected Result:
Common Scheduler should grab a new connection from the pool if it detects that it lost a connection.

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim9: Reported Defect DEF91376: Issues with the item library definitions and XML Migration.
content:
Article ID: D5105
Defect ID: DEF91376
Product: Dimensions CM 9.1.3
Fixed In: Dimensions CM 10.1.3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Description
The XML Migration fails to copy item
revision to a Dimensions Server, on which the Item Libraries have been
defined, if the SDP port is non-default.
The following error
occurs:
Error: Connection refused by ZUCVD62S (10061). Check daemon is
running.
Error: Cannot open connection to host ZUCVD62S
If the
hostname for the item libraries are changed to ZUCVD62S:12021 then the migration
completes successfully.
The problem is that it is not possible to define
an item library node that uses this syntax. Adminconsole hangs when one
attempts this.

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Solution :
Internal
The workaround is to use the following SQL against he basedb before and after
the migration.
Update obj_types
set node_name =
UPPER('&1')
where node_name = UPPER('&2')
and type_flag =
'I';
commit;
exit;

[content]
DEF91376

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim cm 14.4 : installation on Centos with local postgres fails with this message "dm_psql: could not connect to server: Connection refused"
content:
Article ID: S142531

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
14.4 installation fails at this step :
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Dimensions CM 14.4 - PostgreSQL Database connection details
Please enter PostgreSQL connection parameters.
PostgreSQL hostname: [localhost] <hostname>
PostgreSQL port: [5432]
PostgreSQL Database name: [dim14]
PostgreSQL User name: [postgres]
PostgreSQL password:
Press 1 for Next, 2 for Previous, 3 to Cancel or 5 to Redisplay [1]
dm_psql: could not connect to server: Connection refused Is the server running on host "<hostname>" (<ip address>) and accepting TCP/IP connections on port 5432?
[OK]
what is causing this ? stating that :
1_ port 5432 is opened at firewall
2_ login to postgres works
3_ pg_hba.conf file is set correctly following installation requirements described page 60 of unix installation guide

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
In postgres.conf file (located in same directory that pg_hba.conf file),
listen_addresses
parameter is set to localhost by default.
Adding the ip address of the Centos machine to this parameter then stopping/starting postgres fixes the issue when restarting the installation

title: Serena Knowledgebase - KM-Dim7: Error: Failed to connect to the Dimensions Server! Error: Connection refused by (server host name) (10061) Check Status of Dimensions Network Daemon on and attempt reconnection
content:
Article ID: S121866

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
DIM: Error: Failed to connect to the Dimensions Server! Error:
Connection refused by (server host name) (10061) Check Status of Dimensions
Network Daemon on and attempt reconnection

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Check the following points:
UNIX

1) Check to see if the Dimensions Network process is running and is owned by Root.

PCMS_DFS pcms_lc_accesd
ps -ef | grep pcms_lc_accesd

PCMS_SDP Dimensions_listener
ps -ef |grep dimensions_listener

2) Verify that the Network Administration Tool has been correctly updated to Register the DFS Node.
a) Check "Operating System Registration".
b) Check "Network client and server connections".
c) Check the "Network Node Registration".
c.1) If this information is filled out, select the "Network Node" and then select the "Network Objects" and make sure the information is filled out; in otherwords, ensure that pcms_dfs "tcp" or pcms_sdp "standard dimensions protocol" has been entered properly.

3) Confirm the Port Numbers in the "Services" file and the PCMS.CFG file.

a) The port numbers for PCMS_DFS and PCMS_SDP:

PCMS_DFS should default to 670/tcp in both files
PCMS_SDP should default to 671/tcp in both files.

If a different port is being used, make sure its the same in both files.

b) also ensure that services contains LISTENER with a default port of 1521/tcp.

4) In the PCMS_STARTUP script ensure the DFS lines are uncommented.

5) NOTE: ensure there are no TRACE logs enabled in the pcms_startup script

WINDOWS

1) Check to see if the Dimensions services are running:

PVCS Dimensions services (pcms_dfs)
PVCS Dimensions listener services (pcms_sdp)

2) Verify that the Network Administration Tool has been correctly updated to Register the DFS Node.
a) Check "Operating System Registration".
b) Check "Network client and server connections".
c) Check the "Network Node Registration".
c.1) If this information is filled out, select the "Network Node" and then select the "Network Objects" and make sure the information is filled out; in otherwords, ensure that pcms_dfs "tcp" or pcms_sdp "standard dimensions protocol" has been entered properly.

3) Confirm the Port Numbers in the PCMS.CFG file from de server and client.

The port numbers for PCMS_DFS and PCMS_SDP:

PCMS_SERVICE_PCMS_DFS_TCP should default to 670
PCMS_SERVICE_PCMS_SDP_TCP should default to 671

If a different port is being used, make sure its the same in both files.

[content]
DIM: Error: Failed to connect to the Dimensions Server! Error:
Connection refused by (server host name) (10061) Check Status of Dimensions
Network Daemon on and attempt reconnection

Since the server port is different than the

title: Serena Knowledgebase - Dim10-12: Build returns: 19BC1000E Error: SOAP FAULT: SOAP-ENV:Client"No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. ".
content:
Article ID: S139293

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
Running a Build fails with the following error message
19BC1000E Error: SOAP FAULT: SOAP-ENV:Client"No connection could be made because the target machine actively refused it. ".
COR0004737E Error: Dimensions Build Server authentication failed

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
1. The Dimensions CM Server is using a non standard tomcat port.
Ensure the server DM.CFG has the correct non standard Tomcat port, for example the following server is running tomcat on port 8088.
DM_WEB_URL http://stl-sup-sun2:8088
2. The Node for the Workarea has a listener running on a non standard SDP port.
Ensure that under Network Administration in Admin Console has the port explicitly defined as a number. Also ensure that the port number is specified in the Network Node Connection between the server and the Node for the build area.
The Build area should now appear with the correct non standard port number when the area is viewed in under Build Administration.
E.g. The area BLD1 should use the port 680.
BLD1 STL-SUP-SUN2 /export/home/dmsys/paulc/area/bld1/ STL-SUP-SUN2
680
dmsys no

title: Serena Knowledgebase - PRO8 TRKINET Database information is not complete.  Error: SQL state = 08003 (Microsoft) ODBC driver manager Connection Not Open
content:
Article ID: S114848

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
PRO8 TRKINET Database information is not complete. Error: SQL state = 08003 (Microsoft) ODBC driver manager Connection Not Open

[RESOLUTION]
Resolution
Most likely they have defined the database location incorrectly. You can get the DBMS Location from Tracker administrator server definition wizard. Cut and past it into the web configurator exactly as it is shown.
+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
In the Tracker I-Net configuration page enter "<Oracle server name>; PORT=<prot_number>; SID=<sid name>" for the DBMS location using the Oracle Wire driver and/or the Oracle 8 driver.
Additional Note:
To access Tracker I-NET configuration page, select "Start | Program | Tracker | Tracker web client sample Page"
From This page select 'Tracker Web configuration'

title: Serena Knowledgebase - The Orchestration Engine purge utility does not seem purge much - can get a fix null pointer exception
content:
Article ID: D9839
Defect ID: DEF181093
Product: SBM 2009 R3
Fixed In: SBM 2009 R3

[DESCRIPTION]
Description
When running the Orchestration Engine it is possible to get a fix null pointer exception for very large databases when server is low on memory or disk space.
e.g.
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.NullPointerException
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl$TableInfo.access$000(
ProcessRuntimeDataControl.java:968)
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl.queryTableInfo(Proces
sRuntimeDataControl.java:815)
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl.getTableInfo(ProcessR
untimeDataControl.java:722)
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl.confirm(ProcessRuntim
eDataControl.java:459)
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl.purgeTables(ProcessRu
ntimeDataControl.java:95)
at com.serena.oe.dbTools.ProcessRuntimeDataControl.main(ProcessRuntimeDa
taControl.java:53)
The consequence of this can mean not all data was successfully purged and because the utility failed rerunning does not find data to be purged. Foreign keys may also not be reestablshed.
